I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
[exclude-newer]: Workspace level takes precedence over channel
### Checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://github.com/prefix-dev/pixi/releases/latest) of pixi, using `pixi --version`.


### Reproducible example

On current unreleased main:
```toml
[workspace]
channels = [
    { channel = "conda-forge", exclude-newer = "3y"},
    { channel = "bioconda", exclude-newer = "1y"},
    { channel = "https://prefix.dev/github-releases", exclude-newer = "2d"}] # doesn't work
platforms = ["osx-arm64", "linux-64", "linux-aarch64", "osx-64", "win-64"]
# Takes precedence over the channel-level setting
exclude-newer = "1y"

[dependencies]
python = "*"
chainsaw = "*" # from github-releases
```

Results in:
```
❯ pixi lock
Error:   × failed to solve requirements of environment 'default' for platform 'osx-arm64'
  ├─▶   × failed to solve the environment
  │   
  ╰─▶ Cannot solve the request because of: The following packages are incompatible
      └─ chainsaw * cannot be installed because there are no viable options:
         └─ chainsaw 0.2.5 | 0.2.6 | 0.2.7 | 0.2.8 | 0.2.9 | 0.2.10 | 0.2.11 | 0.2.12 | 0.2.13 | 0.2.14 is excluded because the package is uploaded after the cutoff date of 2025-04-08 04:56:48.943953
      UT
```

### Issue description

The `workspace.exclude-newer` overrides the channel version, which I believe to be wrong.

### Expected behavior

It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` IMO.

</pr_description>

     1	use itertools::Itertools;
     2	use pixi_spec::ExcludeNewer;
     3	use rattler_conda_types::NamedChannelOrUrl;
     4	use toml_edit::{Table, Value};
     5	
     6	/// A channel with an optional priority.
     7	/// If the priority is not specified, it is assumed to be 0.
     8	/// The higher the priority, the more important the channel is.
     9	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
    10	pub struct PrioritizedChannel {
    11	    pub channel: NamedChannelOrUrl,
    12	    pub priority: Option<i32>,
    13	    pub exclude_newer: Option<ExcludeNewer>,
    14	}
    15	
    16	impl PrioritizedChannel {
    17	    /// The prioritized channels contain a priority, sort on this priority.
    18	    /// Higher priority comes first. [-10, 1, 0 ,2] -> [2, 1, 0, -10]
    19	    pub fn sort_channels_by_priority<'a, I>(
    20	        channels: I,
    21	    ) -> impl Iterator<Item = &'a NamedChannelOrUrl>
    22	    where
    23	        I: IntoIterator<Item = &'a crate::PrioritizedChannel>,
    24	    {
    25	        channels
    26	            .into_iter()
    27	            .sorted_by(|a, b| {
    28	                let a = a.priority.unwrap_or(0);
    29	                let b = b.priority.unwrap_or(0);
    30	                b.cmp(&a)
    31	            })
    32	            .map(|prioritized_channel| &prioritized_channel.channel)
    33	    }
    34	}
    35	
    36	impl From<NamedChannelOrUrl> for PrioritizedChannel {
    37	    fn from(value: NamedChannelOrUrl) -> Self {
    38	        Self {
    39	            channel: value,
    40	            priority: None,
    41	            exclude_newer: None,
    42	        }
    43	    }
    44	}
    45	
    46	impl From<(NamedChannelOrUrl, Option<i32>)> for PrioritizedChannel {
    47	    fn from((value, prio): (NamedChannelOrUrl, Option<i32>)) -> Self {
    48	        Self {
    49	            channel: value,
    50	            priority: prio,
    51	            exclude_newer: None,
    52	        }
    53	    }
    54	}
    55	
    56	impl From<PrioritizedChannel> for Value {
    57	    fn from(channel: PrioritizedChannel) -> Self {
    58	        match (channel.priority, channel.exclude_newer) {
    59	            (Some(priority), exclude_newer) => {
    60	                let mut table = Table::new().into_inline_table();
    61	                table.insert("channel", channel.channel.to_string().into());
    62	                table.insert("priority", i64::from(priority).into());
    63	                if let Some(exclude_newer) = exclude_newer {
    64	                    table.insert("exclude-newer", exclude_newer.to_string().into());
    65	                }
    66	                Value::InlineTable(table)
    67	            }
    68	            (None, Some(exclude_newer)) => {
    69	                let mut table = Table::new().into_inline_table();
    70	                table.insert("channel", channel.channel.to_string().into());
    71	                table.insert("exclude-newer", exclude_newer.to_string().into());
    72	                Value::InlineTable(table)
    73	            }
    74	            (None, None) => Value::String(toml_edit::Formatted::new(channel.channel.to_string())),
    75	        }
    76	    }
    77	}

    21	/// The name of a feature. This is either a string or default for the default
    22	/// feature.
    23	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
    24	pub struct FeatureName(Cow<'static, str>);
    25	
    26	impl Default for FeatureName {
    27	    fn default() -> Self {
    28	        FeatureName::DEFAULT.clone()
    29	    }
    30	}
    31	
    32	impl Serialize for FeatureName {
    33	    fn serialize<S: serde::Serializer>(&self, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
    34	        serializer.serialize_str(self.as_str())
    35	    }
    36	}
    37	
    38	impl Hash for FeatureName {
    39	    fn hash<H: Hasher>(&self, state: &mut H) {
    40	        self.as_str().hash(state)
    41	    }
    42	}
    43	
    44	impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for FeatureName {
    45	    fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error>
    46	    where
    47	        D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,
    48	    {
    49	        Ok(String::deserialize(deserializer)?.into())
    50	    }
    51	}

    53	impl<'s> From<&'s str> for FeatureName {
    54	    fn from(value: &'s str) -> Self {
    55	        FeatureName(Cow::Owned(value.to_owned()))
    56	    }
    57	}
    58

    60	    fn from(value: String) -> Self {
    61	        Self(Cow::Owned(value))
    62	    }
    63	}
    64	
    65	impl FeatureName {
    66	    pub const DEFAULT: Self = FeatureName(Cow::Borrowed(consts::DEFAULT_FEATURE_NAME));
    67	
    68	    /// Returns the string representation of the feature.
    69	    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
    70	        &self.0
    71	    }
    72	
    73	    /// Returns true if the feature is the default feature.
    74	    pub fn is_default(&self) -> bool {
    75	        self == &Self::DEFAULT
    76	    }
    77	
    78	    /// Returns the name of the feature if it is not default.
    79	    pub fn non_default(&self) -> Option<&str> {
    80	        self.is_default().not().then(|| self.as_str())
    81	    }
    82	}

    86	        self.as_str()

    91	    type Err = Infallible;
    92	
    93	    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
    94	        Ok(FeatureName::from(s))
    95	    }
    96	}
    97	
    98	impl From<FeatureName> for String {
    99	    fn from(name: FeatureName) -> Self {
   100	        name.0.into_owned()
   101	    }
   102	}
   103	impl<'a> From<&'a FeatureName> for String {
   104	    fn from(name: &'a FeatureName) -> Self {
   105	        name.as_str().to_owned()
   106	    }
   107	}
   108	impl fmt::Display for FeatureName {
   109	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   110	        write!(f, "{}", self.as_str())
   111	    }
   112	}
   113	
   114	/// A feature describes a set of functionalities. It allows us to group
   115	/// functionality and its dependencies together.
   116	///
   117	/// Individual features cannot be used directly, instead they are grouped
   118	/// together into environments. Environments are then locked and installed.
   119	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   120	pub struct Feature {
   121	    /// The name of the feature or `None` if the feature is the default feature.
   122	    pub name: FeatureName,
   123	
   124	    /// The platforms this feature is available on.
   125	    ///
   126	    /// This value is `None` if this feature does not specify any platforms and
   127	    /// the default platforms from the project should be used.
   128	    pub platforms: Option<IndexSet<Platform>>,
   129	
   130	    /// Channels specific to this feature.
   131	    ///
   132	    /// This value is `None` if this feature does not specify any channels and
   133	    /// the default channels from the project should be used.
   134	    pub channels: Option<IndexSet<PrioritizedChannel>>,
   135	
   136	    /// Channel priority for the solver, if not set the default is used.
   137	    /// This value is `None` and there are multiple features,
   138	    /// it will be seen as unset and overwritten by a set one.
   139	    pub channel_priority: Option<ChannelPriority>,
   140	
   141	    /// Solve strategy specific for this feature.
   142	    ///
   143	    /// If this value is `None` and there are multiple features,
   144	    /// it will be seen as unset and overwritten by a set one.
   145	    pub solve_strategy: Option<SolveStrategy>,
   146	
   147	    /// Additional system requirements
   148	    pub system_requirements: SystemRequirements,
   149	
   150	    /// Pypi-related options
   151	    pub pypi_options: Option<PypiOptions>,
   152	
   153	    /// Target specific configuration.
   154	    pub targets: Targets<WorkspaceTarget>,
   155	}
   156	
   157	impl Feature {
   158	    /// Construct a new feature with the given name.
   159	    pub fn new(name: FeatureName) -> Self {
   160	        Feature {
   161	            name,
   162	            platforms: None,
   163	            channels: None,
   164	            channel_priority: None,
   165	            solve_strategy: None,
   166	            system_requirements: SystemRequirements::default(),
   167	            pypi_options: None,
   168	            targets: <Targets<WorkspaceTarget> as Default>::default(),
   169	        }
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    /// Returns true if this feature is the default feature.
   173	    pub fn is_default(&self) -> bool {
   174	        self.name.is_default()
   175	    }
   176	
   177	    /// Returns a mutable reference to the platforms of the feature. Create them
   178	    /// if needed
   179	    pub fn platforms_mut(&mut self) -> &mut IndexSet<Platform> {
   180	        self.platforms.get_or_insert_with(Default::default)
   181	    }
   182	
   183	    /// Returns a mutable reference to the channels of the feature. Create them
   184	    /// if needed
   185	    pub fn channels_mut(&mut self) -> &mut IndexSet<PrioritizedChannel> {
   186	        self.channels.get_or_insert_with(Default::default)
   187	    }
   188	
   189	    /// Returns the run dependencies of the target for the given `platform`.
   190	    ///
   191	    /// If the platform is `None` no platform specific dependencies are
   192	    /// returned.
   193	    ///
   194	    /// This function returns `None` if there is not a single feature that has
   195	    /// any dependencies defined.
   196	    pub fn run_dependencies(
   197	        &self,
   198	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   199	    ) -> Option<Cow<'_, DependencyMap<PackageName, PixiSpec>>> {
   200	        self.dependencies(SpecType::Run, platform)
   201	    }
   202	
   203	    /// Returns the host dependencies of the target for the given `platform`.
   204	    ///
   205	    /// If the platform is `None` no platform specific dependencies are
   206	    /// returned.
   207	    ///
   208	    /// This function returns `None` if there is not a single feature that has
   209	    /// any dependencies defined.
   210	    pub fn host_dependencies(
   211	        &self,
   212	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   213	    ) -> Option<Cow<'_, DependencyMap<PackageName, PixiSpec>>> {
   214	        self.dependencies(SpecType::Host, platform)
   215	    }
   216	
   217	    /// Returns the run dependencies of the target for the given `platform`.
   218	    ///
   219	    /// If the platform is `None` no platform specific dependencies are
   220	    /// returned.
   221	    ///
   222	    /// This function returns `None` if there is not a single feature that has
   223	    /// any dependencies defined.
   224	    pub fn build_dependencies(
   225	        &self,
   226	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   227	    ) -> Option<Cow<'_, DependencyMap<PackageName, PixiSpec>>> {
   228	        self.dependencies(SpecType::Build, platform)
   229	    }
   230	
   231	    /// Returns the dependencies of the feature for a given `spec_type` and
   232	    /// `platform`.
   233	    ///
   234	    /// This function returns a [`Cow`]. If the dependencies are not combined or
   235	    /// overwritten by multiple targets than this function returns a
   236	    /// reference to the internal dependencies.
   237	    ///
   238	    /// Returns `None` if this feature does not define any target that has any
   239	    /// of the requested dependencies.
   240	    ///
   241	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
   242	    /// into consideration.
   243	    pub fn dependencies(
   244	        &self,
   245	        spec_type: SpecType,
   246	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   247	    ) -> Option<Cow<'_, DependencyMap<PackageName, PixiSpec>>> {
   248	        self.targets
   249	            .resolve(platform)
   250	            // Get the targets in reverse order, from least specific to most specific.
   251	            // This is required because we want more specific targets to overwrite their specs.
   252	            .rev()
   253	            .filter_map(|t| t.dependencies(spec_type))
   254	            .filter(|deps| !deps.is_empty())
   255	            .fold(None, |acc, deps| match acc {
   256	                None => Some(Cow::Borrowed(deps)),
   257	                Some(acc) => {
   258	                    // Overwrite the accumulator with specs from this target
   259	                    // More specific targets (processed later) overwrite less specific ones
   260	                    Some(Cow::Owned(acc.as_ref().overwrite(deps)))
   261	                }
   262	            })
   263	    }
   264	
   265	    /// Returns the combined dependencies of the feature and `platform`.
   266	    ///
   267	    /// The `build` dependencies overwrite the `host` dependencies which
   268	    /// overwrite the `run` dependencies.
   269	    ///
   270	    /// This function returns a [`Cow`]. If the dependencies are not combined or
   271	    /// overwritten by multiple targets than this function returns a
   272	    /// reference to the internal dependencies.
   273	    ///
   274	    /// Returns `None` if this feature does not define any target that has any
   275	    /// of the requested dependencies.
   276	    ///
   277	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
   278	    /// into consideration.
   279	    pub fn combined_dependencies(
   280	        &self,
   281	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   282	    ) -> Option<Cow<'_, DependencyMap<PackageName, PixiSpec>>> {
   283	        self.targets
   284	            .resolve(platform)
   285	            // Get the targets in reverse order, from least specific to most specific.
   286	            // This is required because we want more specific targets to overwrite their specs.
   287	            .rev()
   288	            .filter_map(|t| t.combined_dependencies())
   289	            .filter(|deps| !deps.is_empty())
   290	            .fold(None, |acc, deps| match acc {
   291	                None => Some(deps),
   292	                Some(acc) => {
   293	                    // Overwrite the accumulator with specs from this target
   294	                    // More specific targets (processed later) overwrite less specific ones
   295	                    Some(Cow::Owned(acc.as_ref().overwrite(deps.as_ref())))
   296	                }
   297	            })
   298	    }
   299	
   300	    /// Returns the PyPi dependencies of the feature for a given `platform`.
   301	    ///
   302	    /// This function returns a [`Cow`]. If the dependencies are not combined or
   303	    /// overwritten by multiple targets than this function returns a
   304	    /// reference to the internal dependencies.
   305	    ///
   306	    /// Returns `None` if this feature does not define any target that has any
   307	    /// of the requested dependencies.
   308	    pub fn pypi_dependencies(
   309	        &self,
   310	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   311	    ) -> Option<Cow<'_, DependencyMap<PypiPackageName, PixiPypiSpec>>> {
   312	        self.targets
   313	            .resolve(platform)
   314	            // Get the targets in reverse order, from least specific to most specific.
   315	            // This is required because we want more specific targets to overwrite their specs.
   316	            .rev()
   317	            .filter_map(|t| t.pypi_dependencies.as_ref())
   318	            .filter(|deps| !deps.is_empty())
   319	            .fold(None, |acc, deps| match acc {
   320	                None => Some(Cow::Borrowed(deps)),
   321	                Some(acc) => {
   322	                    // Overwrite the accumulator with specs from this target
   323	                    // More specific targets (processed later) overwrite less specific ones
   324	                    Some(Cow::Owned(acc.as_ref().overwrite(deps)))
   325	                }
   326	            })
   327	    }
   328	
   329	    /// Returns the activation scripts for the most specific target that matches
   330	    /// the given `platform`.
   331	    ///
   332	    /// Returns `None` if this feature does not define any target with an
   333	    /// activation.
   334	    pub fn activation_scripts(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> Option<&Vec<String>> {
   335	        self.targets
   336	            .resolve(platform)
   337	            .filter_map(|t| t.activation.as_ref())
   338	            .filter_map(|a| a.scripts.as_ref())
   339	            .next()
   340	    }
   341	
   342	    /// Returns the activation environment for the most specific target that
   343	    /// matches the given `platform`.
   344	    ///
   345	    /// Returns `None` if this feature does not define any target with an
   346	    /// activation.
   347	    pub fn activation_env(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> IndexMap<String, String> {
   348	        self.targets
   349	            .resolve(platform)
   350	            .filter_map(|t| t.activation.as_ref())
   351	            .filter_map(|a| a.env.as_ref())
   352	            .fold(IndexMap::new(), |mut acc, x| {
   353	                for (k, v) in x {
   354	                    if !acc.contains_key(k) {
   355	                        acc.insert(k.clone(), v.clone());
   356	                    }
   357	                }
   358	                acc
   359	            })
   360	    }
   361	
   362	    /// Returns true if the feature contains any reference to a pypi
   363	    /// dependencies.
   364	    pub fn has_pypi_dependencies(&self) -> bool {
   365	        self.targets.targets().any(|t| {
   366	            t.pypi_dependencies
   367	                .as_ref()
   368	                .map(|deps| !deps.is_empty())
   369	                .unwrap_or(false)
   370	        })
   371	    }
   372	
   373	    /// Returns any pypi_options if they are set.
   374	    pub fn pypi_options(&self) -> Option<&PypiOptions> {
   375	        self.pypi_options.as_ref()
   376	    }
   377	
   378	    /// Returns true if the feature supports the given platform.
   379	    ///
   380	    /// A feature supports a platform if it has no platform restriction or if
   381	    /// its `platforms` set contains the given platform. If `platform` is
   382	    /// `None`, the feature is always considered supported.
   383	    pub fn supports_platform(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> bool {
   384	        match (&self.platforms, platform) {
   385	            (Some(platforms), Some(p)) => platforms.contains(&p),
   386	            _ => true,
   387	        }
   388	    }
   389	
   390	    /// Returns the dev dependencies of the feature for a given `platform`.
   391	    ///
   392	    /// Dev dependencies are source packages whose build/host/run dependencies
   393	    /// should be installed without building the packages themselves.
   394	    ///
   395	    /// This function returns a [`Cow`]. If the dependencies are not combined or
   396	    /// overwritten by multiple targets than this function returns a
   397	    /// reference to the internal dependencies.
   398	    ///
   399	    /// Returns `None` if this feature does not define any target that has any
   400	    /// of the requested dev dependencies.
   401	    ///
   402	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
   403	    /// into consideration.
   404	    pub fn dev_dependencies(
   405	        &self,
   406	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   407	    ) -> Option<Cow<'_, DependencyMap<PackageName, pixi_spec::SourceSpec>>> {
   408	        self.targets
   409	            .resolve(platform)
   410	            // Get the targets in reverse order, from least specific to most specific.
   411	            // This is required because we want more specific targets to overwrite their specs.
   412	            .rev()
   413	            .filter_map(|t| t.dev_dependencies.as_ref())
   414	            .filter(|deps| !deps.is_empty())
   415	            .fold(None, |acc, deps| match acc {
   416	                None => Some(Cow::Borrowed(deps)),
   417	                Some(acc) => {
   418	                    // Overwrite the accumulator with specs from this target
   419	                    // More specific targets (processed later) overwrite less specific ones
   420	                    Some(Cow::Owned(acc.as_ref().overwrite(deps)))
   421	                }
   422	            })
   423	    }
   424	
   425	    /// Returns the version constraints of the feature for a given `platform`.
   426	    ///
   427	    /// Constraints limit the versions of packages that can be installed
   428	    /// without explicitly requiring them to be installed.
   429	    ///
   430	    /// This function returns a [`Cow`]. If the constraints are not combined or
   431	    /// overwritten by multiple targets than this function returns a
   432	    /// reference to the internal constraints.
   433	    ///
   434	    /// Returns `None` if this feature does not define any target that has any
   435	    /// constraints.
   436	    ///
   437	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific constraints are taken
   438	    /// into consideration.
   439	    pub fn constraints(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> Option<Cow<'_, CondaConstraints>> {
   440	        <response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

    30	/// Holds the parsed content of the workspace part of a pixi manifest. This
    31	/// describes the part related to the workspace only.
    32	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
    33	pub struct WorkspaceManifest {
    34	    /// Information about the project
    35	    pub workspace: Workspace,
    36	
    37	    /// All the features defined in the project.
    38	    pub features: IndexMap<FeatureName, Feature>,
    39	
    40	    /// All the environments defined in the project.
    41	    pub environments: Environments,
    42	
    43	    /// The solve groups that are part of the project.
    44	    pub solve_groups: SolveGroups,
    45	}
    46	
    47	impl WorkspaceManifest {
    48	    /// Parses a TOML string into a [`WorkspaceManifest`].
    49	    pub fn from_toml_str<S: AsRef<str> + SourceCode>(
    50	        source: S,
    51	    ) -> Result<Self, WithSourceCode<TomlError, S>> {
    52	        TomlManifest::from_toml_str(source.as_ref())
    53	            .and_then(|manifest| {
    54	                manifest.into_workspace_manifest(
    55	                    ExternalWorkspaceProperties::default(),
    56	                    PackageDefaults::default(),
    57	                    None,
    58	                )
    59	            })
    60	            .map(|manifests| manifests.0)
    61	            .map_err(|e| WithSourceCode { source, error: e })
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    /// Returns the default feature.
    65	    ///
    66	    /// This is the feature that is added implicitly by the tables at the root
    67	    /// of the project manifest.
    68	    pub fn default_feature(&self) -> &Feature {
    69	        self.features
    70	            .get(&FeatureName::DEFAULT)
    71	            .expect("default feature should always exist")
    72	    }
    73	
    74	    /// Returns a mutable reference to the default feature.
    75	    pub(crate) fn default_feature_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Feature {
    76	        self.features
    77	            .get_mut(&FeatureName::DEFAULT)
    78	            .expect("default feature should always exist")
    79	    }
    80	
    81	    /// Returns the mutable feature with the given name or `Err` if it does not
    82	    /// exist.
    83	    pub fn feature_mut<Q>(&mut self, name: &Q) -> miette::Result<&mut Feature>
    84	    where
    85	        Q: ?Sized + Hash + Equivalent<FeatureName> + Display,
    86	    {
    87	        self.features.get_mut(name).ok_or_else(|| {
    88	            miette!(
    89	                "Feature {} does not exist",
    90	                consts::FEATURE_STYLE.apply_to(name)
    91	            )
    92	        })
    93	    }
    94	
    95	    /// Returns the mutable feature with the given name
    96	    pub fn get_or_insert_feature_mut(&mut self, name: &FeatureName) -> &mut Feature {
    97	        self.features
    98	            .entry(name.clone())
    99	            .or_insert_with(|| Feature::new(name.clone()))
   100	    }
   101	
   102	    /// Returns the default environment
   103	    ///
   104	    /// This is the environment that is added implicitly as the environment with
   105	    /// only the default feature. The default environment can be overwritten
   106	    /// by a environment named `default`.
   107	    pub fn default_environment(&self) -> &Environment {
   108	        let envs = &self.environments;
   109	        envs.find(&EnvironmentName::Named(String::from(
   110	            consts::DEFAULT_ENVIRONMENT_NAME,
   111	        )))
   112	        .or_else(|| envs.find(&EnvironmentName::Default))
   113	        .expect("default environment should always exist")
   114	    }
   115	
   116	    /// Returns the environment with the given name or `None` if it does not
   117	    /// exist.
   118	    pub fn environment<Q>(&self, name: &Q) -> Option<&Environment>
   119	    where
   120	        Q: ?Sized + Hash + Equivalent<EnvironmentName>,
   121	    {
   122	        self.environments.find(name)
   123	    }
   124	
   125	    /// Returns a hashmap of the tasks that should run only the given platform.
   126	    /// If the platform is `None`, only the default targets tasks are
   127	    /// returned.
   128	    pub fn tasks(
   129	        &self,
   130	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   131	        feature_name: &FeatureName,
   132	    ) -> Result<HashMap<TaskName, &Task>, GetFeatureError> {
   133	        Ok(self
   134	            .features
   135	            .get(feature_name)
   136	            // Return error if feature does not exist
   137	            .ok_or(GetFeatureError::FeatureDoesNotExist(feature_name.clone()))?
   138	            .targets
   139	            .resolve(platform)
   140	            .rev()
   141	            .flat_map(|target| target.tasks.iter())
   142	            .map(|(name, task)| (name.clone(), task))
   143	            .collect())
   144	    }
   145	
   146	    /// Returns a mutable reference to a [`WorkspaceTarget`], creating it if
   147	    /// needed.
   148	    pub fn get_or_insert_target_mut(
   149	        &mut self,
   150	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   151	        name: Option<&FeatureName>,
   152	    ) -> &mut WorkspaceTarget {
   153	        let feature = match name {
   154	            Some(feature) => self.get_or_insert_feature_mut(feature),
   155	            None => self.default_feature_mut(),
   156	        };
   157	        feature
   158	            .targets
   159	            .for_opt_target_or_default_mut(platform.map(TargetSelector::from).as_ref())
   160	    }
   161	
   162	    /// Returns a mutable reference to a [`WorkspaceTarget`]. Returns `None` if
   163	    /// the target doesnt exist.
   164	    pub fn target_mut(
   165	        &mut self,
   166	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   167	        name: &FeatureName,
   168	    ) -> Option<&mut WorkspaceTarget> {
   169	        self.feature_mut(name)
   170	            .unwrap()
   171	            .targets
   172	            .for_opt_target_mut(platform.map(TargetSelector::Platform).as_ref())
   173	    }
   174	
   175	    /// Returns the feature with the given name or `None` if it does not exist.
   176	    pub fn feature<Q>(&self, name: &Q) -> Option<&Feature>
   177	    where
   178	        Q: ?Sized + Hash + Equivalent<FeatureName>,
   179	    {
   180	        self.features.get(name)
   181	    }
   182	
   183	    /// Returns the preview field of the project
   184	    pub fn preview(&self) -> &Preview {
   185	        &self.workspace.preview
   186	    }
   187	
   188	    /// Returns true if any of the features has pypi dependencies defined.
   189	    ///
   190	    /// This also returns true if the `pypi-dependencies` key is defined but
   191	    /// empty.
   192	    pub fn has_pypi_dependencies(&self) -> bool {
   193	        self.features
   194	            .values()
   195	            .flat_map(|f| f.targets.targets())
   196	            .any(|f| f.pypi_dependencies.is_some())
   197	    }
   198	
   199	    /// Returns default values for the external package properties.
   200	    pub(crate) fn workspace_package_properties(&self) -> WorkspacePackageProperties {
   201	        WorkspacePackageProperties {
   202	            name: self.workspace.name.clone(),
   203	            version: self.workspace.version.clone(),
   204	            description: self.workspace.description.clone(),
   205	            repository: self.workspace.repository.clone(),
   206	            license: self.workspace.license.clone(),
   207	            license_file: self.workspace.license_file.clone(),
   208	            readme: self.workspace.readme.clone(),
   209	            authors: self.workspace.authors.clone(),
   210	            documentation: self.workspace.documentation.clone(),
   211	            homepage: self.workspace.homepage.clone(),
   212	        }
   213	    }
   214	}
   215	
   216	/// A mutable context that allows modifying the workspace manifest both in
   217	/// memory and on disk.
   218	pub struct WorkspaceManifestMut<'a> {
   219	    pub workspace: &'a mut WorkspaceManifest,
   220	    pub document: &'a mut ManifestDocument,
   221	}
   222	
   223	impl WorkspaceManifestMut<'_> {
   224	    /// Add a task to the project.
   225	    ///
   226	    /// This function modifies both the workspace and the TOML document. Use
   227	    /// `ManifestProvenance::save` to persist the changes to disk.
   228	    pub fn add_task(
   229	        &mut self,
   230	        name: TaskName,
   231	        task: Task,
   232	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   233	        feature_name: &FeatureName,
   234	    ) -> miette::Result<()> {
   235	        // Check if the task already exists
   236	        if let Ok(tasks) = self.workspace.tasks(platform, feature_name)
   237	            && tasks.contains_key(&name)
   238	        {
   239	            miette::bail!("task {} already exists", name);
   240	        }
   241	
   242	        // Add the task to the Toml manifest
   243	        self.document
   244	            .add_task(name.as_str(), task.clone(), platform, feature_name)?;
   245	
   246	        // Add the task to the manifest
   247	        self.workspace
   248	            .get_or_insert_target_mut(platform, Some(feature_name))
   249	            .tasks
   250	            .insert(name, task);
   251	
   252	        Ok(())
   253	    }
   254	
   255	    /// Remove a task from the project.
   256	    ///
   257	    /// This function modifies both the workspace and the TOML document. Use
   258	    /// `ManifestProvenance::save` to persist the changes to disk.
   259	    pub fn remove_task(
   260	        &mut self,
   261	        name: TaskName,
   262	        platform: Option<Platform>,
   263	        feature_name: &FeatureName,
   264	    ) -> miette::Result<()> {
   265	        // Check if the task exists
   266	        self.workspace
   267	            .tasks(platform, feature_name)?
   268	            .get(&name)
   269	            .ok_or_else(|| miette::miette!("task {} does not exist", name))?;
   270	
   271	        // Remove the task from the Toml manifest
   272	        self.document
   273	            .remove_task(name.as_str(), platform, feature_name)?;
   274	
   275	        // Remove the task from the internal manifest
   276	        self.workspace
   277	            .feature_mut(feature_name)?
   278	            .targets
   279	            .for_opt_target_mut(platform.map(TargetSelector::from).as_ref())
   280	            .map(|target| target.tasks.remove(&name));
   281	
   282	        Ok(())
   283	    }
   284	
   285	    /// Adds an environment to the workspace. Overwrites the entry if it already
   286	    /// exists.
   287	    ///
   288	    /// This function modifies both the workspace and the TOML document. Use
   289	    /// `ManifestProvenance::save` to persist the changes to disk.
   290	    pub fn add_environment(
   291	        &mut self,
   292	        name: String,
   293	        features: Option<Vec<String>>,
   294	        solve_group: Option<String>,
   295	        no_default_feature: bool,
   296	    ) -> miette::Result<()> {
   297	        // Make sure the features exist
   298	        for feature in features.iter().flatten() {
   299	            if self.workspace.features.get(feature.as_str()).is_none() {
   300	                return Err(UnknownFeature::new(feature.to_string(), &*self.workspace).into());
   301	            }
   302	        }
   303	
   304	        self.document.add_environment(
   305	            name.clone(),
   306	            features.clone(),
   307	            solve_group.clone(),
   308	            no_default_feature,
   309	        )?;
   310	
   311	        let environment_idx = self.workspace.environments.add(Environment {
   312	            name: EnvironmentName::Named(name),
   313	            features: features.unwrap_or_default(),
   314	            solve_group: None,
   315	            no_default_feature,
   316	        });
   317	
   318	        if let Some(solve_group) = solve_group {
   319	            self.workspace
   320	                .solve_groups
   321	                .add(solve_group, environment_idx);
   322	        }
   323	
   324	        Ok(())
   325	    }
   326	
   327	    /// Removes an environment from the project.
   328	    ///
   329	    /// This function modifies both the workspace and the TOML document. Use
   330	    /// `ManifestProvenance::save` to persist the changes to disk.
   331	    pub fn remove_environment(&mut self, name: &str) -> miette::Result<bool> {
   332	        // Remove the environment from the TOML document
   333	        if !self.document.remove_environment(name)? {
   334	            return Ok(false);
   335	        }
   336	
   337	        // Remove the environment from the internal manifest
   338	        let environment_idx = self
   339	            .workspace
   340	            .environments
   341	            .by_name
   342	            .shift_remove(name)
   343	            .expect("environment should exist");
   344	
   345	        // Remove the environment from the solve groups
   346	        self.workspace
   347	            .solve_groups
   348	            .iter_mut()
   349	            .for_each(|group| group.environments.retain(|&idx| idx != environment_idx));
   350	
   351	        Ok(true)
   352	    }
   353	
   354	    /// Removes a feature from the project. The feature is automatically
   355	    /// removed from all environments that use it.
   356	    ///
   357	    /// This function modifies both the workspace and the TOML document. Use
   358	    /// `ManifestProvenance::save` to persist the changes to disk.
   359	    ///
   360	    /// Returns the list of environments that were modified.
   361	    pub fn remove_feature(
   362	        &mut self,
   363	        feature_name: &FeatureName,
   364	    ) -> miette::Result<Vec<EnvironmentName>> {
   365	        if feature_name.is_default() {
   366	            miette::bail!("Cannot remove the default feature");
   367	        }
   368	
   369	        if self.workspace.features.get(feature_name).is_none() {
   370	            tracing::warn!("Feature `{}` doesn't exist", feature_name);
   371	            return Ok(Vec::new());
   372	        }
   373	
   374	        self.workspace.features.shift_remove(feature_name);
   375	
   376	        // Find all environments that use this feature and update them
   377	        let environments_using_feature: Vec<_> = self
   378	            .workspace
   379	            .environments
   380	            .iter()
   381	            .filter(|env| env.features.contains(&feature_name.to_string()))
   382	            .cloned()
   383	            .collect();
   384	
   385	        for env in &environments_using_feature {
   386	            let updated_features: Vec<String> = env
   387	                .features
   388	                .iter()
   389	                .filter(|f| f.as_str() != feature_name.to_string())
   390	                .cloned()
   391	                .collect();
   392	
   393	            let solve_group = env
   394	                .solve_group
   395	                .map(|idx| self.workspace.solve_groups[idx].name.clone());
   396	
   397	            // Update the environment, minus the removed feature
   398	            self.document.add_environment(
   399	                env.name.to_string(),
   400	                Some(updated_features.clone()),
   401	                solve_group.clone(),
   402	                env.no_default_feature,
   403	            )?;
   404	
   405	            let environment_idx = self.workspace.environments.add(Environment {
   406	                name: env.name.clone(),
   407	                features: updated_features,
   408	                solve_group: None,
   409	                no_default_feature: env.no_default_feature,
   410	            });
   411	
   412	            if let Some(solve_group) = solve_group {
   413	                self.workspace
   414	                    .solve_groups
   415	                    .add(solve_group, environment_idx);
   416	            }
   417	        }
   418	
   419	        // Remove the feature from the TOML document
   420	        self.document.remove_feature(feature_name)?;
   421	
   422	        let modified_environments = environments_using_feature
   423	            .iter()
   424	            .map(|env| env.name.clone())
   425	            .collect();
   426	
   427	        Ok(modified_environments)
   428	    }
   429	
   430	    /// Add a platform to the project
   431	    ///
   432	    /// This function modifies both the workspace and the TOML document. Use
   433	    /// `ManifestProvenance::save` to persist the changes to disk.
   434	    pub fn add_platforms<'a>(
   435	        &mut self,
   436	        platforms: impl Iterator<Item = &'a Platform> + Clone,
   437	        feature_name: &FeatureName,
   438	    ) -> miette::Result<()> {
   439	        // Get current and new platforms for the feature
   440	        let current = if feature_name.is_default() {
   441	            &mut self.workspace.workspace.platforms
   442	        } else {
   443	            self.workspace
   444	                .get_or_insert_feature_mut(feature_name)
   445	                .platforms_mut()
   446	        };
   447	        let to_add: IndexSet<_> = platforms.cloned().collect();
   448	        let new: IndexSet<_> = to_add.difference(current).cloned().collect();
   449	
   450	        // Add the platforms to the manifest
   451	        current.extend(new.clone());
   452	
   453	        // Then to the TOML document
   454	        let platforms = self.document.get_array_mut("platforms", feature_name)?;
   455	        for platform in new.iter() {
   456	            platforms.push(platform.to_string());
   457	        }
   458	
   459	        Ok(())
   460	    }
   461	
   462	    /// Remove the platform(s) from the project
   463	    ///
   464	    /// This function modifies both the worksp<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

    23	/// ChannelPriorityCombination error, thrown when multiple channel priorities
    24	/// are set
    25	#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error, Diagnostic)]
    26	#[error("Multiple channel priorities are not allowed in a single environment")]
    27	pub struct ChannelPriorityCombinationError;
    28	
    29	/// A trait that implement various methods for collections that combine
    30	/// attributes of Features It is implemented by Environment, GroupedEnvironment
    31	/// and SolveGroup. Remove some of the boilerplate of combining features and its
    32	/// derived data from multiple sources.
    33	///
    34	/// The name of the lifetime parameter is named `'source` that the borrow comes
    35	/// from the source of the data for most implementations this will be the pixi
    36	/// project.
    37	///
    38	/// There is blanket implementation available for all types that implement
    39	/// [`HasWorkspaceManifest`] and [`HasFeaturesIter`]
    40	pub trait FeaturesExt<'source>: HasWorkspaceManifest<'source> + HasFeaturesIter<'source> {
    41	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection, preserving the
    42	    /// effective metadata for the first winning entry of every unique channel.
    43	    fn prioritized_channels(
    44	        &self,
    45	    ) -> IndexMap<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl, &'source PrioritizedChannel> {
    46	        let channels = self.features().flat_map(|feature| match &feature.channels {
    47	            Some(channels) => channels,
    48	            None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.channels,
    49	        });
    50	
    51	        let mut prioritized = IndexMap::new();
    52	        for channel in channels.into_iter().sorted_by(|a, b| {
    53	            let a = a.priority.unwrap_or(0);
    54	            let b = b.priority.unwrap_or(0);
    55	            b.cmp(&a)
    56	        }) {
    57	            prioritized.entry(&channel.channel).or_insert(channel);
    58	        }
    59	
    60	        prioritized
    61	    }
    62	
    63	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection.
    64	    ///
    65	    /// Users can specify custom channels on a per-feature basis. This method
    66	    /// collects and deduplicates all the channels from all the features in
    67	    /// the order they are defined in the manifest.
    68	    ///
    69	    /// If a feature does not specify any channel the default channels from the
    70	    /// project metadata are used instead.
    71	    fn channels(&self) -> IndexSet<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl> {
    72	        self.prioritized_channels().into_keys().collect()
    73	    }
    74	
    75	    /// Returns the channels associated with this collection.
    76	    ///
    77	    /// This function is similar to [`Self::channels]` but it resolves the
    78	    /// channel urls using the provided channel config.
    79	    fn channel_urls(
    80	        &self,
    81	        channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
    82	    ) -> Result<Vec<ChannelUrl>, ParseChannelError> {
    83	        self.channels()
    84	            .into_iter()
    85	            .cloned()
    86	            .map(|channel| channel.into_base_url(channel_config))
    87	            .collect()
    88	    }
    89	
    90	    /// Returns the channel priority, error on multiple, different values, return None if
    91	    /// no value is set.
    92	    ///
    93	    /// When using multiple channel priorities over different features we should
    94	    /// error as the user should decide what they want.
    95	    fn channel_priority(&self) -> Result<Option<ChannelPriority>, ChannelPriorityCombinationError> {
    96	        let mut channel_priority = None;
    97	        for feature in self.features() {
    98	            if let Some(priority) = feature.channel_priority {
    99	                // If we already have a priority and it's different, error
   100	                if channel_priority.is_some() && channel_priority != Some(priority) {
   101	                    return Err(ChannelPriorityCombinationError);
   102	                }
   103	                channel_priority = Some(priority);
   104	            }
   105	        }
   106	        Ok(channel_priority)
   107	    }
   108	
   109	    /// Returns the raw workspace exclude-newer configuration before channel and
   110	    /// package-specific overrides are applied.
   111	    fn exclude_newer_raw(&self) -> Option<ExcludeNewer> {
   112	        self.workspace_manifest().workspace.exclude_newer
   113	    }
   114	
   115	    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration.
   116	    fn exclude_newer_config(
   117	        &self,
   118	    ) -> Result<Option<rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
   119	        self.exclude_newer_config_resolved()
   120	            .map(|exclude_newer| exclude_newer.map(Into::into))
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
   124	    fn exclude_newer_config_resolved(
   125	        &self,
   126	    ) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
   127	        let mut exclude_newer = self
   128	            .exclude_newer_raw()
   129	            .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));
   130	
   131	        for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
   132	            let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
   133	                continue;
   134	            };
   135	
   136	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
   137	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
   138	            });
   139	
   140	            *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
   141	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
   142	                    .clone()
   143	                    .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
   144	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
   145	                    channel.channel.to_string(),
   146	                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
   147	                ),
   148	            };
   149	        }
   150	
   151	        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
   152	            .workspace_manifest()
   153	            .workspace
   154	            .exclude_newer_package_overrides
   155	        {
   156	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
   157	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
   158	            });
   159	
   160	            *config = match package_exclude_newer {
   161	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
   162	                    config.clone().with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), *dt)
   163	                }
   164	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config
   165	                    .clone()
   166	                    .with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff()),
   167	            };
   168	        }
   169	
   170	        Ok(exclude_newer)
   171	    }
   172	
   173	    /// Returns the effective PyPI exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
   174	    fn pypi_exclude_newer_config_resolved(&self) -> ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer {
   175	        let mut exclude_newer = self
   176	            .exclude_newer_raw()
   177	            .map(|config| ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()))
   178	            .unwrap_or_default();
   179	
   180	        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
   181	            .workspace_manifest()
   182	            .workspace
   183	            .pypi_exclude_newer_package_overrides
   184	        {
   185	            exclude_newer = match package_exclude_newer {
   186	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
   187	                    exclude_newer.with_package_cutoff(name.as_normalized().clone(), *dt)
   188	                }
   189	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => exclude_newer.with_package_cutoff(
   190	                    name.as_normalized().clone(),
   191	                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff(),
   192	                ),
   193	            };
   194	        }
   195	
   196	        exclude_newer
   197	    }
   198	
   199	    /// Returns the strategy for solving packages.
   200	    ///
   201	    /// The chosen strategy is the first explicitly declared one in a feature
   202	    /// as they are provided by the [`HasFeaturesIter::features`] iterator.
   203	    ///
   204	    /// If no feature declares a strategy, the default value of [`SolveStrategy`] is used.
   205	    fn solve_strategy(&self) -> SolveStrategy {
   206	        self.features()
   207	            .flat_map(|feature| feature.solve_strategy)
   208	            .next()
   209	            .unwrap_or_default()
   210	    }
   211	
   212	    /// Returns the platforms that this collection is compatible with.
   213	    ///
   214	    /// Which platforms a collection support depends on which platforms the
   215	    /// selected features of the collection supports. The platforms that are
   216	    /// supported by the collection is the intersection of the platforms
   217	    /// supported by its features.
   218	    ///
   219	    /// Features can specify which platforms they support through the
   220	    /// `platforms` key. If a feature does not specify any platforms the
   221	    /// features defined by the project are used.
   222	    fn platforms(&self) -> HashSet<Platform> {
   223	        self.features()
   224	            .map(|feature| {
   225	                match &feature.platforms {
   226	                    Some(platforms) => platforms,
   227	                    None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.platforms,
   228	                }
   229	                .iter()
   230	                .copied()
   231	                .collect::<HashSet<_>>()
   232	            })
   233	            .reduce(|accumulated_platforms, feat| {
   234	                accumulated_platforms.intersection(&feat).copied().collect()
   235	            })
   236	            .unwrap_or_default()
   237	    }
   238	
   239	    /// Returns the system requirements for this collection.
   240	    ///
   241	    /// The system requirements of the collection are the union of the system
   242	    /// requirements of all the features in the collection. If multiple
   243	    /// features specify a requirement for the same system package, the
   244	    /// highest is chosen.
   245	    fn local_system_requirements(&self) -> SystemRequirements {
   246	        self.features()
   247	            .map(|feature| &feature.system_requirements)
   248	            .fold(SystemRequirements::default(), |acc, req| {
   249	                acc.union(req)
   250	                    .expect("system requirements should have been validated upfront")
   251	            })
   252	    }
   253	
   254	    /// Returns true if any of the features has any reference to a pypi
   255	    /// dependency.
   256	    fn has_pypi_dependencies(&self) -> bool {
   257	        self.features().any(|f| f.has_pypi_dependencies())
   258	    }
   259	
   260	    /// Returns the PyPi dependencies to install for this collection.
   261	    ///
   262	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
   263	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
   264	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
   265	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
   266	    fn pypi_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> PyPiDependencies {
   267	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
   268	            .features()
   269	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
   270	            .filter_map(|f| f.pypi_dependencies(platform))
   271	            .collect();
   272	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
   273	    }
   274	
   275	    /// Returns the dependencies to install for this collection.
   276	    ///
   277	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
   278	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
   279	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
   280	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
   281	    ///
   282	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
   283	    /// into consideration.
   284	    fn dependencies(&self, kind: SpecType, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDependencies {
   285	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
   286	            .features()
   287	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
   288	            .filter_map(|f| f.dependencies(kind, platform))
   289	            .collect();
   290	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
   291	    }
   292	
   293	    /// Returns the combined dependencies to install for this collection.
   294	    ///
   295	    /// The `build` dependencies overwrite the `host` dependencies which
   296	    /// overwrite the `run` dependencies.
   297	    ///
   298	    /// The dependencies of all features are combined. This means that if two
   299	    /// features define a requirement for the same package that both
   300	    /// requirements are returned. The different requirements per package
   301	    /// are sorted in the same order as the features they came from.
   302	    ///
   303	    /// If the `platform` is `None` no platform specific dependencies are taken
   304	    /// into consideration.
   305	    fn combined_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDependencies {
   306	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
   307	            .features()
   308	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
   309	            .filter_map(|f| f.combined_dependencies(platform))
   310	            .collect();
   311	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
   312	    }
   313	
   314	    /// Returns the combined dev dependencies to install for this collection.
   315	    ///
   316	    /// Dev dependencies from all features in the group are collected and
   317	    /// merged. If multiple features define the same dev dependency, the
   318	    /// last one wins (later features override earlier ones).
   319	    fn combined_dev_dependencies(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaDevDependencies {
   320	        let deps: Vec<_> = self
   321	            .features()
   322	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
   323	            .filter_map(|f| f.dev_dependencies(platform))
   324	            .collect();
   325	
   326	        DependencyMap::merge_all(deps.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
   327	    }
   328	
   329	    /// Returns the combined version constraints for this collection.
   330	    ///
   331	    /// Constraints from all features are combined. If multiple features define
   332	    /// a constraint for the same package, all constraints are retained and the
   333	    /// solver must satisfy all of them simultaneously.
   334	    ///
   335	    /// If the `platform` is `None`, no platform specific constraints are taken
   336	    /// into consideration.
   337	    fn combined_constraints(&self, platform: Option<Platform>) -> CondaConstraints {
   338	        let constraints: Vec<_> = self
   339	            .features()
   340	            .filter(|f| f.supports_platform(platform))
   341	            .filter_map(|f| f.constraints(platform))
   342	            .collect();
   343	        DependencyMap::merge_all(constraints.iter().map(|d| d.as_ref()))
   344	    }
   345	
   346	    /// Returns the pypi options for this collection.
   347	    ///
   348	    /// The pypi options of all features are combined. They will be combined in
   349	    /// the order that they are defined in the manifest.
   350	    /// The index-url is a special case and can only be defined once. This
   351	    /// should have been verified beforehand.
   352	    fn pypi_options(&self) -> PypiOptions {
   353	        // Collect all the pypi-options from the features in one set,
   354	        // deduplicate them and sort them on feature index, default feature comes last.
   355	        let pypi_options: Vec<_> = self
   356	            .features()
   357	            .filter_map(|feature| {
   358	                if feature.pypi_options().is_none() {
   359	                    self.workspace_manifest().workspace.pypi_options.as_ref()
   360	                } else {
   361	                    feature.pypi_options()
   362	                }
   363	            })
   364	            .collect();
   365	
   366	        // Merge all the pypi options into one.
   367	        pypi_options
   368	            .into_iter()
   369	            .fold(PypiOptions::default(), |acc, opts| {
   370	                acc.union(opts)
   371	                    .expect("merging of pypi-options should already have been checked")
   372	            })
   373	    }
   374	}

2026-05-30 18:37:08,619 - INFO - swea-agent-prefix-dev__pixi-5850 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I can see the issue! Look at the `exclude_newer_config_resolved` function (lines 124-171). The logic is:

1. Line 127-129: Start with `workspace.exclude_newer` (the global workspace-level setting)
2. Lines 131-149: Then iterate over channels and apply channel-level `exclude_newer` overrides

The problem is that when the workspace-level `exclude_newer` is set, it creates a `ResolvedExcludeNewer` with that datetime as the base cutoff. Then when channel-level settings are processed, the `get_or_insert_with` on line 136 will NOT trigger because `exclude_newer` is already `Some(...)`. However, the channel-level overrides use `config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(...)`, which should still work.

Wait, let me look more carefully at the `ExcludeNewer` and `ResolvedExcludeNewer` types to understand the semantics.

     1	use chrono::{DateTime, Days, NaiveDate, NaiveTime, Utc};
     2	use rattler_conda_types::PackageName;
     3	use std::{collections::BTreeMap, str::FromStr};
     4	
     5	/// Specifies how to exclude newer packages from the solve.
     6	///
     7	/// Can be either:
     8	/// - An absolute timestamp
     9	/// - A relative duration (e.g., `7d`, `1h`, `30m`, `1h30m`)
    10	#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
    11	pub enum ExcludeNewer {
    12	    /// An absolute point in time. Packages newer than this are excluded.
    13	    Timestamp(DateTime<Utc>),
    14	    /// A relative duration. At solve time, packages newer than `now - duration`
    15	    /// are excluded.
    16	    Duration(std::time::Duration),
    17	}
    18	
    19	/// A fully resolved exclude-newer configuration with absolute cutoffs.
    20	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
    21	#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
    22	pub struct ResolvedExcludeNewer {
    23	    /// The default cutoff date. Packages uploaded after this date are excluded.
    24	    pub cutoff: DateTime<Utc>,
    25	
    26	    /// Channel-specific cutoff dates that override [`Self::cutoff`] for
    27	    /// records from matching channels.
    28	    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "BTreeMap::is_empty")]
    29	    pub channel_cutoffs: BTreeMap<String, DateTime<Utc>>,
    30	
    31	    /// Package-specific cutoff dates that override both [`Self::cutoff`] and
    32	    /// [`Self::channel_cutoffs`] for matching package names.
    33	    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "BTreeMap::is_empty")]
    34	    pub package_cutoffs: BTreeMap<PackageName, DateTime<Utc>>,
    35	
    36	    /// Whether to include packages that don't have a timestamp.
    37	    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "std::ops::Not::not")]
    38	    pub include_unknown_timestamp: bool,
    39	}
    40	
    41	impl ExcludeNewer {
    42	    /// Returns the effective cutoff for the current time.
    43	    pub fn cutoff(&self) -> DateTime<Utc> {
    44	        match self {
    45	            Self::Timestamp(cutoff) => *cutoff,
    46	            Self::Duration(duration) => {
    47	                let duration = chrono::Duration::from_std(*duration)
    48	                    .expect("exclude-newer duration is too large");
    49	                Utc::now() - duration
    50	            }
    51	        }
    52	    }
    53	}
    54	
    55	impl ResolvedExcludeNewer {
    56	    /// Creates a new configuration from an absolute cutoff date.
    57	    pub fn from_datetime(cutoff: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {
    58	        Self {
    59	            cutoff,
    60	            channel_cutoffs: BTreeMap::new(),
    61	            package_cutoffs: BTreeMap::new(),
    62	            include_unknown_timestamp: false,
    63	        }
    64	    }
    65	
    66	    /// Adds a channel-specific cutoff override.
    67	    pub fn with_channel_cutoff(
    68	        mut self,
    69	        channel: impl Into<String>,
    70	        cutoff: DateTime<Utc>,
    71	    ) -> Self {
    72	        self.channel_cutoffs.insert(channel.into(), cutoff);
    73	        self
    74	    }
    75	
    76	    /// Adds a package-specific cutoff override.
    77	    pub fn with_package_cutoff(mut self, package: PackageName, cutoff: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {
    78	        self.package_cutoffs.insert(package, cutoff);
    79	        self
    80	    }
    81	}
    82	
    83	impl From<ResolvedExcludeNewer> for rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer {
    84	    fn from(value: ResolvedExcludeNewer) -> Self {
    85	        let mut config = rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer::from_datetime(value.cutoff)
    86	            .with_include_unknown_timestamp(value.include_unknown_timestamp);
    87	
    88	        for (channel, cutoff) in value.channel_cutoffs {
    89	            config = config.with_channel_cutoff(channel, cutoff);
    90	        }
    91	
    92	        for (package, cutoff) in value.package_cutoffs {
    93	            config = config.with_package_cutoff(package, cutoff);
    94	        }
    95	
    96	        config
    97	    }
    98	}
    99	
   100	impl FromStr for ExcludeNewer {
   101	    type Err = String;
   102	
   103	    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   104	        parse_exclude_newer_str(s)
   105	    }
   106	}
   107	
   108	impl std::fmt::Display for ExcludeNewer {
   109	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   110	        match self {
   111	            ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => dt.fmt(f),
   112	            ExcludeNewer::Duration(dur) => humantime::format_duration(*dur).fmt(f),
   113	        }
   114	    }
   115	}
   116	
   117	impl serde::Serialize for ExcludeNewer {
   118	    fn serialize<S>(&self, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error>
   119	    where
   120	        S: serde::Serializer,
   121	    {
   122	        match self {
   123	            Self::Timestamp(cutoff) => cutoff.serialize(serializer),
   124	            Self::Duration(duration) => {
   125	                serializer.collect_str(&humantime::Duration::from(*duration))
   126	            }
   127	        }
   128	    }
   129	}
   130	
   131	impl<'de> serde::Deserialize<'de> for ExcludeNewer {
   132	    fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error>
   133	    where
   134	        D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,
   135	    {
   136	        #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
   137	        #[serde(untagged)]
   138	        enum RawExcludeNewer {
   139	            Timestamp(DateTime<Utc>),
   140	            Duration(String),
   141	        }
   142	
   143	        match RawExcludeNewer::deserialize(deserializer)? {
   144	            RawExcludeNewer::Timestamp(cutoff) => Ok(ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(cutoff)),
   145	            RawExcludeNewer::Duration(value) => {
   146	                parse_exclude_newer_str(&value).map_err(serde::de::Error::custom)
   147	            }
   148	        }
   149	    }
   150	}
   151	
   152	fn parse_exclude_newer_str(s: &str) -> Result<ExcludeNewer, String> {
   153	    if let Ok(duration) = s.parse::<humantime::Duration>() {
   154	        return Ok(ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration.into()));
   155	    }
   156	
   157	    let date_err = match NaiveDate::parse_from_str(s, "%Y-%m-%d") {
   158	        Ok(date) => {
   159	            let next_midnight = date
   160	                .checked_add_days(Days::new(1))
   161	                .expect("valid exclude-newer date should have a following day")
   162	                .and_time(NaiveTime::MIN)
   163	                .and_utc();
   164	            return Ok(ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(next_midnight));
   165	        }
   166	        Err(err) => err,
   167	    };
   168	
   169	    let timestamp_err = match DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s) {
   170	        Ok(timestamp) => return Ok(ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(timestamp.with_timezone(&Utc))),
   171	        Err(err) => err,
   172	    };
   173	
   174	    Err(format!(
   175	        "`{s}` is neither a valid duration, date ({date_err}), nor timestamp ({timestamp_err})"
   176	    ))
   177	}
   178	
   179	#[cfg(test)]
   180	mod test {
   181	    use super::*;
   182	    use serde_json::json;
   183	
   184	    #[test]
   185	    fn test_from_str_timestamp() {
   186	        assert_eq!(
   187	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-02T00:00:00Z").unwrap(),
   188	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-02T00:00:00+00:00").unwrap(),
   189	        );
   190	
   191	        match (
   192	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-02T00:00:00Z").unwrap(),
   193	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-02T00:00:00+00:00").unwrap(),
   194	        ) {
   195	            (ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(a), ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(b)) => assert_eq!(a, b),
   196	            _ => panic!("expected timestamps"),
   197	        }
   198	    }
   199	
   200	    #[test]
   201	    fn test_from_str_date() {
   202	        assert_eq!(
   203	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-02").unwrap(),
   204	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-03T00:00:00Z").unwrap(),
   205	        );
   206	    }
   207	
   208	    #[test]
   209	    fn test_from_str_duration() {
   210	        assert_eq!(
   211	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("7d").unwrap(),
   212	            ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(7 * 24 * 60 * 60)),
   213	        );
   214	        assert_eq!(
   215	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("1h").unwrap(),
   216	            ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(60 * 60)),
   217	        );
   218	        assert_eq!(
   219	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("30m").unwrap(),
   220	            ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30 * 60)),
   221	        );
   222	        assert_eq!(
   223	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("1h30m").unwrap(),
   224	            ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(90 * 60)),
   225	        );
   226	        assert_eq!(
   227	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("7days").unwrap(),
   228	            ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(7 * 24 * 60 * 60)),
   229	        );
   230	    }
   231	
   232	    #[test]
   233	    fn test_from_str_invalid_reports_supported_formats() {
   234	        let err = ExcludeNewer::from_str("date").unwrap_err();
   235	        assert!(err.contains("valid duration"), "got: {err}");
   236	        assert!(err.contains("date ("), "got: {err}");
   237	        assert!(err.contains("timestamp ("), "got: {err}");
   238	    }
   239	
   240	    #[test]
   241	    fn test_cutoff_for_duration_is_relative_to_now() {
   242	        let before = Utc::now();
   243	        let cutoff = ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(60 * 60)).cutoff();
   244	        let after = Utc::now();
   245	
   246	        assert!(
   247	            cutoff >= before - chrono::Duration::hours(1) - chrono::Duration::seconds(1),
   248	            "cutoff {cutoff} should be close to one hour before {before}",
   249	        );
   250	        assert!(
   251	            cutoff <= after - chrono::Duration::hours(1) + chrono::Duration::seconds(1),
   252	            "cutoff {cutoff} should be close to one hour before {after}",
   253	        );
   254	    }
   255	
   256	    #[test]
   257	    fn test_serde_deserializes_timestamp_with_space_separator() {
   258	        let parsed: ExcludeNewer = serde_json::from_value(json!("2006-12-02 02:07:43Z")).unwrap();
   259	
   260	        assert_eq!(
   261	            parsed,
   262	            ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-02T02:07:43Z").unwrap()
   263	        );
   264	    }
   265	
   266	    #[test]
   267	    fn test_serde_roundtrips_duration() {
   268	        let value = ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(90 * 60));
   269	
   270	        let serialized = serde_json::to_value(value).unwrap();
   271	        assert_eq!(serialized, json!("1h 30m"));
   272	
   273	        let deserialized: ExcludeNewer = serde_json::from_value(serialized).unwrap();
   274	        assert_eq!(deserialized, value);
   275	    }
   276	
   277	    #[test]
   278	    fn test_display_duration() {
   279	        let d = ExcludeNewer::Duration(std::time::Duration::from_secs(7 * 24 * 60 * 60));
   280	        let display = format!("{d}");
   281	        assert_eq!(display, "7days");
   282	    }
   283	
   284	    #[test]
   285	    fn test_display_timestamp() {
   286	        let t = ExcludeNewer::from_str("2006-12-02T02:07:43Z").unwrap();
   287	        let display = format!("{t}");
   288	        assert!(display.contains("2006"), "got: {display}");
   289	    }
   290	
   291	    #[test]
   292	    fn test_resolved_into_rattler_solve_preserves_overrides() {
   293	        let default_cutoff = DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2006-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   294	            .unwrap()
   295	            .with_timezone(&Utc);
   296	        let channel_cutoff = DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2006-12-03T02:07:43Z")
   297	            .unwrap()
   298	            .with_timezone(&Utc);
   299	        let package_cutoff = DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2006-12-04T02:07:43Z")
   300	            .unwrap()
   301	            .with_timezone(&Utc);
   302	
   303	        let config: rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer =
   304	            ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(default_cutoff)
   305	                .with_channel_cutoff("https://prefix.dev/conda-forge", channel_cutoff)
   306	                .with_package_cutoff(PackageName::new_unchecked("foo"), package_cutoff)
   307	                .into();
   308	
   309	        assert_eq!(
   310	            config.cutoff_for_package(&PackageName::new_unchecked("baz"), None),
   311	            default_cutoff
   312	        );
   313	        assert_eq!(
   314	            config.cutoff_for_package(
   315	                &PackageName::new_unchecked("bar"),
   316	                Some("https://prefix.dev/conda-forge"),
   317	            ),
   318	            channel_cutoff
   319	        );
   320	        assert_eq!(
   321	            config.cutoff_for_package(
   322	                &PackageName::new_unchecked("foo"),
   323	                Some("https://prefix.dev/conda-forge"),
   324	            ),
   325	            package_cutoff
   326	        );
   327	    }
   328	}

2026-05-30 18:37:36,512 - INFO - swea-agent-prefix-dev__pixi-5850 - ========================= STEP 18 =========================
2026-05-30 18:37:36,516 - DEBUG - swea-lm-prefix-dev__pixi-5850 - n_cache_control: 2
2026-05-30 18:37:54,746 - DEBUG - swea-lm-prefix-dev__pixi-5850 - Response: ModelResponse(id='782cb3fe652c49bbb33d5d2dd37cdf64', created=1780166257, model='DeepSeek-V4-Pro', object='chat.completion', system_fingerprint=None, choices=[Choices(finish_reason='tool_calls', index=0, message=Message(content='Now let me understand the PR issue more clearly. The PR says:\n\n> The `workspace.exclude-newer` overrides the channel version, which I believe to be wrong.\n> It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` IMO.\n\nWait, re-reading: "It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` IMO."\n\nThis seems to say the order of precedence should be:\n1. `workspace.exclude-newer` (LOWEST precedence, i.e., most general)\n2. `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` (MIDDLE precedence)\n3. `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` (HIGHEST precedence, i.e., per-package overrides)\n\nWait, that doesn\'t match. Let me re-read: "The `workspace.exclude-newer` overrides the channel version, which I believe to be wrong."\n\nSo the bug is: when you set both `workspace.exclude-newer` and a channel-level `exclude-newer`, the workspace-level one takes precedence (overrides) the channel-level one. The PR author says it should be the opposite: channel-level should take precedence over workspace-level.\n\nBut looking at the expected behavior: "It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` IMO."\n\nHmm, the `>` notation could mean precedence (higher on left wins) or could mean "greater" in scope. Let me look at the example from the PR:\n\n```toml\n[workspace]\nchannels = [\n    { channel = "conda-forge", exclude-newer = "3y"},\n    { channel = "bioconda", exclude-newer = "1y"},\n    { channel = "https://prefix.dev/github-releases", exclude-newer = "2d"}] # doesn\'t work\nplatforms = ["osx-arm64", "linux-64", "linux-aarch64", "osx-64", "win-64"]\n# Takes precedence over the channel-level setting\nexclude-newer = "1y"\n\n[dependencies]\npython = "*"\nchainsaw = "*" # from github-releases\n```\n\nThe issue is that `chainsaw` from `github-releases` channel has a channel-level `exclude-newer = "2d"` which should allow packages from the last 2 days, but the workspace-level `exclude-newer = "1y"` overrides it, making it 1 year. The author expects the channel-level setting to take precedence over the workspace-level setting.\n\nBut wait, "2d" is more restrictive than "1y". If `exclude-newer = "2d"` means "exclude packages newer than 2 days ago", and `exclude-newer = "1y"` means "exclude packages newer than 1 year ago", then the workspace-level `1y` is actually LESS restrictive (allows more packages). But the error says:\n\n```\nchainsaw * cannot be installed because there are no viable options:\n   └─ chainsaw 0.2.5 | ... is excluded because the package is uploaded after the cutoff date of 2025-04-08 04:56:48.943953\n```\n\nThe cutoff date is `2025-04-08`, which is about 1 year from now (assuming now is ~2025). So the workspace-level `1y` is being used instead of the channel-level `2d`.\n\nNow let me trace through the code in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`:\n\n```rust\nfn exclude_newer_config_resolved(\n    &self,\n) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {\n    let mut exclude_newer = self\n        .exclude_newer_raw()  // workspace.exclude_newer = "1y"\n        .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));  // Some with cutoff = now - 1y\n    \n    for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {\n        let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {\n            continue;\n        };\n        \n        let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {\n            ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)\n        });\n        \n        *config = match channel_exclude_newer {\n            ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config\n                .clone()\n                .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),\n            ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(\n                channel.channel.to_string(),\n                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n    // ... package overrides ...\n}\n```\n\nSo `exclude_newer` starts as `Some(ResolvedExcludeNewer { cutoff: now - 1y, ... })`.
