    62	#[derive(Debug, Error, Diagnostic)]
    63	pub enum EnvironmentUnsat {
    64	    #[error("the channels in the lock-file do not match the environments channels")]
    65	    ChannelsMismatch,
    66	
    67	    #[error("channels were extended with additional lower-priority channels")]
    68	    ChannelsExtended,
    69	
    70	    #[error("platform(s) '{platforms}' present in the lock-file but not in the environment", platforms = .0.iter().map(|p| p.as_str()).join(", ")
    71	    )]
    72	    AdditionalPlatformsInLockFile(HashSet<Platform>),
    73	
    74	    #[error(transparent)]
    75	    IndexesMismatch(#[from] IndexesMismatch),
    76	
    77	    #[error(transparent)]
    78	    InvalidChannel(#[from] ParseChannelError),
    79	
    80	    #[error(transparent)]
    81	    InvalidDistExtensionInNoBuild(#[from] ExtensionError),
    82	
    83	    #[error(
    84	        "the lock-file contains non-binary package: '{0}', but the pypi-option `no-build` is set"
    85	    )]
    86	    NoBuildWithNonBinaryPackages(String),
    87	
    88	    #[error(
    89	        "the lock-file was solved with a different strategy ({locked_strategy}) than the one selected ({expected_strategy})",
    90	        locked_strategy = fmt_solve_strategy(*.locked_strategy),
    91	        expected_strategy = fmt_solve_strategy(*.expected_strategy),
    92	    )]
    93	    SolveStrategyMismatch {
    94	        locked_strategy: rattler_solve::SolveStrategy,
    95	        expected_strategy: rattler_solve::SolveStrategy,
    96	    },
    97	
    98	    #[error(
    99	        "the lock-file was solved with a different channel priority ({locked_priority}) than the one selected ({expected_priority})",
   100	        locked_priority = fmt_channel_priority(*.locked_priority),
   101	        expected_priority = fmt_channel_priority(*.expected_priority),
   102	    )]
   103	    ChannelPriorityMismatch {
   104	        locked_priority: rattler_solve::ChannelPriority,
   105	        expected_priority: rattler_solve::ChannelPriority,
   106	    },
   107	
   108	    #[error(
   109	        "the lock-file was solved with a different PyPI prerelease mode ({locked_mode}) than the one selected ({expected_mode})"
   110	    )]
   111	    PypiPrereleaseModeMismatch {
   112	        locked_mode: PrereleaseMode,
   113	        expected_mode: PrereleaseMode,
   114	    },
   115	    #[error(
   116	        "the lock-file was solved with system requirements incompatible with the tags on wheel ({wheel})"
   117	    )]
   118	    PypiWheelTagsMismatch { wheel: String },
   119	
   120	    #[error(transparent)]
   121	    ExcludeNewerMismatch(#[from] ExcludeNewerMismatch),
   122	}
   123	
   124	fn fmt_channel_priority(priority: rattler_solve::ChannelPriority) -> &'static str {
   125	    match priority {
   126	        rattler_solve::ChannelPriority::Strict => "strict",
   127	        rattler_solve::ChannelPriority::Disabled => "disabled",
   128	    }
   129	}
   130	
   131	fn fmt_solve_strategy(strategy: rattler_solve::SolveStrategy) -> &'static str {
   132	    match strategy {
   133	        rattler_solve::SolveStrategy::Highest => "highest",
   134	        rattler_solve::SolveStrategy::LowestVersion => "lowest-version",
   135	        rattler_solve::SolveStrategy::LowestVersionDirect => "lowest-version-direct",
   136	    }
   137	}
   138	
   139	#[derive(Debug, Error)]
   140	pub struct ExcludeNewerMismatch {
   141	    package: String,
   142	    timestamp: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
   143	    exclude_newer: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
   144	}
   145	
   146	impl Display for ExcludeNewerMismatch {
   147	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   148	        write!(
   149	            f,
   150	            "the locked package '{}' has timestamp {}, which is newer than the environment's exclude-newer cutoff {}",
   151	            self.package, self.timestamp, self.exclude_newer
   152	        )
   153	    }
   154	}
   155	
   156	fn verify_exclude_newer(
   157	    environment: &Environment<'_>,
   158	    locked_environment: &rattler_lock::Environment<'_>,
   159	) -> Result<(), ExcludeNewerMismatch> {
   160	    for (_platform, packages) in locked_environment.conda_packages_by_platform() {
   161	        let Some(exclude_newer) = environment
   162	            .exclude_newer_config()
   163	            .expect("environment channels were already validated")
   164	        else {
   165	            continue;
   166	        };
   167	
   168	        for package in packages {
   169	            let record = package.record();
   170	            let channel = package
   171	                .as_binary()
   172	                .and_then(|binary| binary.channel.as_ref())
   173	                .map(ToString::to_string);
   174	
   175	            if let Some(timestamp) = record.timestamp.as_ref()
   176	                && exclude_newer.is_excluded(&record.name, channel.as_deref(), Some(timestamp))
   177	            {
   178	                return Err(ExcludeNewerMismatch {
   179	                    package: record.name.as_source().to_string(),
   180	                    timestamp: (*timestamp).into(),
   181	                    exclude_newer: exclude_newer
   182	                        .cutoff_for_package(&record.name, channel.as_deref()),
   183	                });
   184	            }
   185	        }
   186	    }
   187	
   188	    Ok(())
   189	}
   190	
   191	#[derive(Debug, Error)]
   192	pub struct IndexesMismatch {
   193	    current: PypiIndexes,
   194	    previous: Option<PypiIndexes>,
   195	}
   196	
   197	impl Display for IndexesMismatch {
   198	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   199	        if let Some(previous) = &self.previous {
   200	            write!(
   201	                f,
   202	                "the indexes used to previously solve to lock file do not match the environments indexes.\n \
   203	                Expected: {expected:#?}\n Found: {found:#?}",
   204	                expected = previous,
   205	                found = self.current
   206	            )
   207	        } else {
   208	            write!(
   209	                f,
   210	                "the indexes used to previously solve to lock file are missing"
   211	            )
   212	        }
   213	    }
   214	}
   215	
   216	#[derive(Debug, Error)]
   217	pub struct SourceTreeHashMismatch {
   218	    pub computed: PackageHashes,
   219	    pub locked: Option<PackageHashes>,
   220	}
   221	
   222	impl Display for SourceTreeHashMismatch {
   223	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   224	        let computed_hash = self
   225	            .computed
   226	            .sha256()
   227	            .map(|hash| format!("{hash:x}"))
   228	            .or(self.computed.md5().map(|hash| format!("{hash:x}")));
   229	        let locked_hash = self.locked.as_ref().and_then(|hash| {
   230	            hash.sha256()
   231	                .map(|hash| format!("{hash:x}"))
   232	                .or(hash.md5().map(|hash| format!("{hash:x}")))
   233	        });
   234	
   235	        match (computed_hash, locked_hash) {
   236	            (None, None) => write!(f, "could not compute a source tree hash"),
   237	            (Some(computed), None) => {
   238	                write!(
   239	                    f,
   240	                    "the computed source tree hash is '{computed}', but the lock-file does not contain a hash"
   241	                )
   242	            }
   243	            (Some(computed), Some(locked)) => write!(
   244	                f,
   245	                "the computed source tree hash is '{computed}', but the lock-file contains '{locked}'"
   246	            ),
   247	            (None, Some(locked)) => write!(
   248	                f,
   249	                "could not compute a source tree hash, but the lock-file contains '{locked}'"
   250	            ),
   251	        }
   252	    }
   253	}

   256	pub enum PlatformUnsat {
   257	    #[error("the requirement '{0}' could not be satisfied (required by '{1}')")]
   258	    UnsatisfiableMatchSpec(Box<MatchSpec>, String),

   264	    RequiredSourceIsBinary(String, String),

   267	    RequiredBinaryIsSource(String),

   270	    SourcePackageMismatch(String, SourceMismatchError),
   271

   273	    FailedToConvertRequirement(pep508_rs::PackageName, #[source] Box<ParsedUrlError>),

   275	    #[error("the requirement '{0}' could not be satisfied (required by '{1}')")]
   276	    UnsatisfiableRequirement(Box<uv_distribution_types::Requirement>, String),
   277	
   278	    #[error("the conda package does not satisfy the pypi requirement '{0}' (required by '{1}')")]
   279	    CondaUnsatisfiableRequirement(Box<uv_distribution_types::Requirement>, String),
   280	
   281	    #[error("there was a duplicate entry for '{0}'")]
   282	    DuplicateEntry(String),

   284	    #[error("the requirement '{0}' failed to parse")]
   285	    FailedToParseMatchSpec(String, #[source] ParseMatchSpecError),
   286

   288	    )]

   290

   295	    CorruptedEntry(String, ParseLockFileError),

   298	    )]
   299	    TooManyPypiPackages(Vec<pep508_rs::PackageName>),
   300	
   301	    #[error("there are PyPi dependencies but a python interpreter is missing from the lock-file")]
   302	    MissingPythonInterpreter,
   303	
   304	    #[error(
   305	        "a marker environment could not be derived from the python interpreter in the lock-file"
   306	    )]
   307	    FailedToDetermineMarkerEnvironment(#[source] Box<dyn Diagnostic + Send + Sync>),
   308	
   309	    #[error(
   310	        "'{0}' requires python version {1} but the python interpreter in the lock-file has version {2}"
   311	    )]
   312	    PythonVersionMismatch(
   313	        pep508_rs::PackageName,
   314	        VersionSpecifiers,
   315	        Box<pep440_rs::Version>,
   316	    ),
   317	
   318	    #[error("when converting {0} into a pep508 requirement")]
   319	    AsPep508Error(pep508_rs::PackageName, #[source] AsPep508Error),
   320	
   321	    #[error("editable pypi dependency on conda resolved package '{0}' is not supported")]
   322	    EditableDependencyOnCondaInstalledPackage(
   323	        uv_normalize::PackageName,
   324	        Box<uv_distribution_types::RequirementSource>,
   325	    ),
   326	
   327	    #[error("direct pypi url dependency to a conda installed package '{0}' is not supported")]
   328	    DirectUrlDependencyOnCondaInstalledPackage(uv_normalize::PackageName),
   329	
   330	    #[error("git dependency on a conda installed package '{0}' is not supported")]
   331	    GitDependencyOnCondaInstalledPackage(uv_normalize::PackageName),
   332	
   333	    #[error("path dependency on a conda installed package '{0}' is not supported")]
   334	    PathDependencyOnCondaInstalledPackage(uv_normalize::PackageName),
   335	
   336	    #[error("directory dependency on a conda installed package '{0}' is not supported")]
   337	    DirectoryDependencyOnCondaInstalledPackage(uv_normalize::PackageName),
   338	
   339	    #[error(
   340	        "the editable package '{0}' was expected to be a directory but is a url, which cannot be editable: '{1}'"
   341	    )]
   342	    EditablePackageIsUrl(uv_normalize::PackageName, String),
   343	
   344	    #[error("the editable package path '{0}', lock does not equal spec path '{1}' == '{2}'")]
   345	    EditablePackagePathMismatch(uv_normalize::PackageName, PathBuf, PathBuf),
   346	
   347	    #[error("failed to determine pypi source tree hash for {0}")]
   348	    FailedToDetermineSourceTreeHash(pep508_rs::PackageName, std::io::Error),
   349	
   350	    #[error("source tree hash for {0} does not match the hash in the lock-file")]
   351	    SourceTreeHashMismatch(pep508_rs::PackageName, #[source] SourceTreeHashMismatch),
   352	
   353	    #[error("the path '{0}, cannot be canonicalized")]
   354	    FailedToCanonicalizePath(PathBuf, #[source] std::io::Error),
   355	
   356	    #[error("expect pypi package name '{expected}' but found '{found}'")]
   357	    LockedPyPINamesMismatch { expected: String, found: String },
   358	
   359	    #[error(
   360	        "'{name}' with specifiers '{specifiers}' does not match the locked version '{version}' "
   361	    )]
   362	    LockedPyPIVersionsMismatch {
   363	        name: String,
   364	        specifiers: String,
   365	        version: String,
   366	    },
   367	
   368	    #[error("the direct url should start with `direct+` or `git+` but found '{0}'")]
   369	    LockedPyPIMalformedUrl(Url),
   370	
   371	    #[error("the spec for '{0}' required a direct url but it was not locked as such")]
   372	    LockedPyPIRequiresDirectUrl(String),
   373	
   374	    #[error("'{name}' has mismatching url: '{spec_url} != {lock_url}'")]
   375	    LockedPyPIDirectUrlMismatch {

2026-05-31 08:45:17,550 - INFO - swea-agent-prefix-dev__pixi-5850 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now let me look more at the `exclude_newer_config_resolved` method and understand the current logic. Let me trace through the issue:

The current code in `features_ext.rs` lines 124-171:

```rust
fn exclude_newer_config_resolved(
    &self,
) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
    let mut exclude_newer = self
        .exclude_newer_raw()          // workspace-level exclude-newer
        .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));

    for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
        let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
            continue;
        };

        let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
            ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
        });

        *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
            ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
                .clone()
                .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
            ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
                channel.channel.to_string(),
                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
            ),
        };
    }

    for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
        .workspace_manifest()
        .workspace
        .exclude_newer_package_overrides
    {
        let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
            ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
        });

        *config = match package_exclude_newer {
            ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
                config.clone().with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), *dt)
            }
            ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config
                .clone()
                .with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff()),
        };
    }

    Ok(exclude_newer)
}
```

The issue: 
1. First, `exclude_newer` is set from workspace-level `exclude_newer` if it exists (line 127-129)
2. Then, channel-level settings add per-channel `channel_cutoffs` (lines 131-149)
3. Then, package-level overrides add per-package `package_cutoffs` (lines 151-168)

But the PR description says:
- Workspace-level `exclude-newer` = `"1y"` (this should be the global default)
- Channel-level for `github-releases` = `"2d"` (this should define a channel-specific cutoff)
- The user expects channel `github-releases` to use the `"2d"` cutoff

But currently, the workspace-level `"1y"` sets a global cutoff that applies to all channels, and then the channel-level `"2d"` adds an additional per-channel cutoff. Since the global cutoff is `1y` (which is ~1 year ago from now) and the channel cutoff is `2d` (2 days ago), the channel cutoff is actually MORE restrictive (newer). Wait, that's confusing.

Actually wait, re-reading the error:
```
chainsaw 0.2.5 | ... | 0.2.14 is excluded because the package is uploaded after the cutoff date of 2025-04-08 04:56:48.943953
```

The cutoff date is `2025-04-08`. If the workspace-level `exclude-newer = "1y"` and the current date is around April 2026 (since PR seems recent), that would be about 1 year ago from now which is around April 2025. So the workspace-level setting is being applied globally. But the channel-level setting was `2d` (2 days), which should have allowed packages within the last 2 days.

So the issue is: the workspace-level `exclude-newer` is being used as the global default, and then channel-level settings are supposed to override it for their specific channels. But looking at the `ResolvedExcludeNewer` struct and how it converts to `rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer`:

The `ResolvedExcludeNewer` has:
- `cutoff`: default cutoff for all packages
- `channel_cutoffs`: per-channel overrides
- `package_cutoffs`: per-package overrides

And in the conversion to `rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer`, the default cutoff is set first, then channel overrides are added. The `rattler_solve` crate's `ExcludeNewer` presumably respects the channel-specific cutoff when a channel is provided (i.e., when checking a package from a specific channel, it uses the channel-specific cutoff if available, otherwise falls back to the default).

So the logic seems correct: workspace-level sets the default, channel-level adds overrides for specific channels. The channel-level `2d` should override the workspace-level `1y` for the `github-releases` channel.

But wait, the PR says this doesn't work. Let me look at how `prioritized_channels` works in `features_ext.rs`:

```rust
fn prioritized_channels(
    &self,
) -> IndexMap<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl, &'source PrioritizedChannel> {
    let channels = self.features().flat_map(|feature| match &feature.channels {
        Some(channels) => channels,
        None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.channels,
    });

    let mut prioritized = IndexMap::new();
    for channel in channels.into_iter().sorted_by(|a, b| {
        let a = a.priority.unwrap_or(0);
        let b = b.priority.unwrap_or(0);
        b.cmp(&a)
    }) {
        prioritized.entry(&channel.channel).or_insert(channel);
    }

    prioritized
}
```

This returns an `IndexMap` where the key is the channel URL and the value is the first `PrioritizedChannel` encountered (after sorting by priority). The `PrioritizedChannel` contains the `exclude_newer` for that channel.

Now in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`:
```rust
for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
    let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
        continue;
    };

    let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
        ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
    });

    *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
        ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
            .clone()
            .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
        ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
            channel.channel.to_string(),
            ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
        ),
    };
}
```

The problem is on this line:
```rust
*config = match channel_exclude_newer {
    ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
        .clone()
        .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
    ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
        channel.channel.to_string(),
        ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
    ),
};
```

This *replaces* the entire config with a new one that has the same channel_cutoffs as before plus the new one. But wait, this should work correctly because `with_channel_cutoff` adds to the existing channel_cutoffs.

But hold on - the channel is represented as `channel.channel.to_string()`. The `channel.channel` is a `NamedChannelOrUrl`. Let me check what `to_string()` returns for this type.

The issue might be that the channel name used in `channel.channel.to_string()` doesn't match the channel name that the solver uses when looking up packages. Let me check...

Actually, let me re-read the PR description more carefully:

```toml

    { channel = "conda-forge", exclude-newer = "3y"},
```

   807	    fn test_channel_specific_exclude_newer_across_multiple_features() {
   808	        let workspace = Workspace::from_str(
   809	            Path::new("pixi.toml"),
   810	            r#"
   811	        [project]
   812	        name = "test"
   813	        channels = ["conda-forge"]
   814	        platforms = ["linux-64"]
   815	        exclude-newer = "2015-12-02T02:07:43Z"
   816	
   817	        [feature.foo]
   818	        channels = [
   819	            { channel = "bioconda", exclude-newer = "2016-12-02T02:07:43Z" },
   820	            { channel = "pytorch", exclude-newer = "2017-12-02T02:07:43Z" },
   821	        ]
   822	
   823	        [feature.bar]
   824	        channels = [
   825	            { channel = "nvidia", exclude-newer = "2018-12-02T02:07:43Z" },
   826	            { channel = "dglteam", exclude-newer = "2019-12-02T02:07:43Z" },
   827	        ]
   828	
   829	        [environments]
   830	        combined = ["foo", "bar"]
   831	        "#,
   832	        )
   833	        .unwrap();
   834	
   835	        let env = workspace.environment("combined").unwrap();
   836	        let config = env.exclude_newer_config().unwrap().unwrap();
   837	        let package = PackageName::new_unchecked("polars");
   838	
   839	        assert_eq!(
   840	            config.cutoff_for_package(&package, Some("bioconda")),
   841	            chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2016-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   842	                .unwrap()
   843	                .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc)
   844	        );
   845	        assert_eq!(
   846	            config.cutoff_for_package(&package, Some("pytorch")),
   847	            chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2017-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   848	                .unwrap()
   849	                .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc)
   850	        );
   851	        assert_eq!(
   852	            config.cutoff_for_package(&package, Some("nvidia")),
   853	            chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2018-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   854	                .unwrap()
   855	                .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc)
   856	        );
   857	        assert_eq!(
   858	            config.cutoff_for_package(&package, Some("dglteam")),
   859	            chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2019-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   860	                .unwrap()
   861	                .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc)
   862	        );

   865	            chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2015-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   866	                .unwrap()
   867	                .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc)

   896	        "#,
   897	        )
   898	        .unwrap();

   915	                .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc)
   916	        );

   918	            config.cutoff_for_package(&package, Some("bioconda")),
   919	            chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2017-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   920	                .unwrap()
   921	                .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc)

   931	    #[test]
   932	    fn test_exclude_newer_package_channel_workspace_precedence() {
   933	        let workspace = Workspace::from_str(
   934	            Path::new("pixi.toml"),
   935	            r#"
   936	        [workspace]
   937	        name = "test"
   938	        channels = [{ channel = "my-private-forge", exclude-newer = "2016-12-02T02:07:43Z" }, "conda-forge"]
   939	        platforms = ["linux-64"]
   940	        exclude-newer = "2015-12-02T02:07:43Z"
   941	
   942	        [dependencies]
   943	        polars = "*"
   944	        numpy = "*"
   945	
   946	        [exclude-newer]
   947	        polars = "2017-12-02T02:07:43Z"
   948	        openssl = "2018-12-02T02:07:43Z"
   949	        "#,
   950	        )
   951	        .unwrap();
   952	
   953	        let env = workspace.environment("default").unwrap();
   954	        let config = env.exclude_newer_config().unwrap().unwrap();
   955	        let polars = PackageName::new_unchecked("polars");
   956	        let numpy = PackageName::new_unchecked("numpy");
   957	        let openssl = PackageName::new_unchecked("openssl");
   958	        let workspace_cutoff = chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2015-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   959	            .unwrap()
   960	            .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc);
   961	        let channel_cutoff = chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2016-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   962	            .unwrap()
   963	            .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc);
   964	        let dependency_cutoff = chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2017-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   965	            .unwrap()
   966	            .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc);
   967	        let constraint_cutoff = chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339("2018-12-02T02:07:43Z")
   968	            .unwrap()
   969	            .with_timezone(&chrono::Utc);
   970	
   971	        let polars_cutoff = config.cutoff_for_package(&polars, Some("my-private-forge"));
   972	        assert_eq!(polars_cutoff, dependency_cutoff);
   973	
   974	        let openssl_cutoff = config.cutoff_for_package(&openssl, Some("my-private-forge"));
   975	        assert_eq!(openssl_cutoff, constraint_cutoff);
   976	
   977	        let private_numpy_cutoff = config.cutoff_for_package(&numpy, Some("my-private-forge"));
   978	        assert_eq!(private_numpy_cutoff, channel_cutoff);
   979	
   980	        let forge_numpy_cutoff = config.cutoff_for_package(&numpy, Some("conda-forge"));
   981	        assert_eq!(forge_numpy_cutoff, workspace_cutoff);
   982	    }

Now I see the test `test_exclude_newer_package_channel_workspace_precedence` (line 932) which tests the expected precedence:

- Workspace-level: `"2015-12-02T02:07:43Z"` (oldest, most restrictive)
- Channel-level for `my-private-forge`: `"2016-12-02T02:07:43Z"` 
- Package-level for `polars`: `"2017-12-02T02:07:43Z"` (newest, most permissive)

The test asserts:
- `polars` on `my-private-forge` → `"2017-12-02"` (package override wins)
- `openssl` on `my-private-forge` → `"2018-12-02"` (package override wins)
- `numpy` on `my-private-forge` → `"2016-12-02"` (channel override wins over workspace)
- `numpy` on `conda-forge` → `"2015-12-02"` (workspace default)

So the expected precedence is: **package-level > channel-level > workspace-level**. That's exactly what the PR description says should happen. And the test shows it's working.

But wait, the PR says it's NOT working. Let me look more carefully at the test...

The test uses `[workspace]` section (new format), while the PR description shows `[workspace]` section too. But the issue might be about how channels are resolved when using the new `[workspace]` format vs old `[project]` format.

Let me check the `test_exclude_newer_package_channel_workspace_precedence` test more carefully. It uses `[workspace]` and the channel `my-private-forge` is a named channel. But the PR description uses `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` which is a URL.

Let me look at how `NamedChannelOrUrl`'s `to_string()` works and how channel matching is done in `rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer::cutoff_for_package`:

pub enum NamedChannelOrUrl {
    /// A named channel
    Name(String),

    /// A url
    Url(Url),

    /// A path. Can either be absolute or relative.
    Path(Utf8TypedPathBuf),
}

impl NamedChannelOrUrl {
    /// Returns the string representation of the channel.
    ///
    /// This method ensures that if the channel is a url, it does not end with a
    /// `/`.
    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
        match self {
            NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name) => name,
            NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url) => url.as_str().trim_end_matches('/'),
            NamedChannelOrUrl::Path(path) => path.as_str(),
        }
    }

    /// Converts the channel to a base url using the given configuration.
    /// This method ensures that the base url always ends with a `/`.
    pub fn into_base_url(self, config: &ChannelConfig) -> Result<ChannelUrl, ParseChannelError> {
        let url = match self {
            NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name) => {
                let mut base_url = config.channel_alias.clone();
                if let Ok(mut segments) = base_url.path_segments_mut() {

     1	//! `rattler_solve` is a crate that provides functionality to solve Conda
     2	//! environments. It currently exposes the functionality through the
     3	//! [`SolverImpl::solve`] function.
     4	
     5	#![deny(missing_docs)]
     6	
     7	#[cfg(feature = "libsolv_c")]
     8	pub mod libsolv_c;
     9	#[cfg(feature = "resolvo")]
    10	pub mod resolvo;
    11	
    12	use std::collections::HashMap;
    13	use std::fmt;
    14	
    15	use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};

    17	    utils::TimestampMs, GenericVirtualPackage, MatchSpec, PackageName, RepoDataRecord, SolverResult,

    20	/// Represents a solver implementation, capable of solving [`SolverTask`]s
    21	pub trait SolverImpl {
    22	    /// The repo data associated to a channel and platform combination
    23	    type RepoData<'a>: SolverRepoData<'a>;
    24	
    25	    /// Resolve the dependencies and return the [`RepoDataRecord`]s that should
    26	    /// be present in the environment.
    27	    fn solve<
    28	        'a,
    29	        R: IntoRepoData<'a, Self::RepoData<'a>>,
    30	        TAvailablePackagesIterator: IntoIterator<Item = R>,
    31	    >(
    32	        &mut self,
    33	        task: SolverTask<TAvailablePackagesIterator>,
    34	    ) -> Result<SolverResult, SolveError>;
    35	}
    36	
    37	/// Represents an error when solving the dependencies for a given environment
    38	#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)]
    39	pub enum SolveError {
    40	    /// There is no set of dependencies that satisfies the requirements
    41	    Unsolvable(Vec<String>),
    42	
    43	    /// The solver backend returned operations that we dont know how to install.
    44	    /// Each string is a somewhat user-friendly representation of which
    45	    /// operation was not recognized and can be used for error reporting
    46	    UnsupportedOperations(Vec<String>),
    47	
    48	    /// Error when converting matchspec
    49	    #[error(transparent)]
    50	    ParseMatchSpecError(#[from] rattler_conda_types::ParseMatchSpecError),
    51	
    52	    /// Encountered duplicate records in the available packages.
    53	    DuplicateRecords(String),
    54	
    55	    /// To support Resolvo cancellation
    56	    Cancelled,
    57	}
    58	
    59	impl fmt::Display for SolveError {
    60	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
    61	        match self {
    62	            SolveError::Unsolvable(operations) => {
    63	                write!(
    64	                    f,
    65	                    "Cannot solve the request because of: {}",
    66	                    operations.join(", ")
    67	                )
    68	            }
    69	            SolveError::UnsupportedOperations(operations) => {
    70	                write!(f, "Unsupported operations: {}", operations.join(", "))
    71	            }
    72	            SolveError::ParseMatchSpecError(e) => {
    73	                write!(f, "Error parsing match spec: {e}")
    74	            }
    75	            SolveError::Cancelled => {
    76	                write!(f, "Solve operation has been cancelled")
    77	            }
    78	            SolveError::DuplicateRecords(filename) => {
    79	                write!(f, "encountered duplicate records for {filename}")
    80	            }
    81	        }
    82	    }
    83	}
    84	
    85	/// Configuration for filtering packages newer than a cutoff.
    86	///
    87	/// This feature helps reduce the risk of installing compromised packages by
    88	/// delaying the installation of newly published versions. In most cases,
    89	/// malicious releases are discovered and removed from channels within a short
    90	/// time window (often within an hour). By requiring packages to have been
    91	/// published for a minimum duration, you give the community time to identify
    92	/// and report malicious packages before they can be installed.
    93	///
    94	/// This is similar to pnpm's `minimumReleaseAge` feature.
    95	///
    96	/// # Example
    97	///
    98	/// ```
    99	/// use std::time::Duration;
   100	/// use rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer;
   101	///
   102	/// // Only allow packages that have been published for at least 1 hour
   103	/// let config = ExcludeNewer::from_duration(Duration::from_secs(60 * 60))
   104	///     // But allow "my-internal-package" to use a package-specific cutoff
   105	///     .with_package_duration("my-internal-package".parse().unwrap(), Duration::ZERO)
   106	///     // And allow a trusted internal channel to skip the delay entirely
   107	///     .with_channel_duration("my-internal-channel", Duration::ZERO);
   108	/// ```
   109	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
   110	pub struct ExcludeNewer {
   111	    /// The default cutoff date. Packages uploaded after this date are excluded.
   112	    cutoff: DateTime<Utc>,
   113	
   114	    /// Channel-specific cutoff dates that override [`Self::cutoff`] for
   115	    /// records from matching channels.
   116	    ///
   117	    /// The key is matched against [`RepoDataRecord::channel`] exactly.
   118	    channel_cutoffs: HashMap<String, DateTime<Utc>>,
   119	
   120	    /// Package-specific cutoff dates that override both [`Self::cutoff`] and
   121	    /// [`Self::channel_cutoffs`] for matching package names.
   122	    package_cutoffs: HashMap<PackageName, DateTime<Utc>>,
   123	
   124	    /// Whether to include packages that don't have a timestamp.
   125	    include_unknown_timestamp: bool,
   126	}
   127	
   128	impl ExcludeNewer {
   129	    fn cutoff_from_duration(duration: std::time::Duration, now: DateTime<Utc>) -> DateTime<Utc> {
   130	        let duration =
   131	            chrono::Duration::from_std(duration).expect("exclude_newer duration is too large");
   132	        now - duration
   133	    }
   134	
   135	    /// Creates a new configuration from an absolute cutoff date.
   136	    pub fn from_datetime(cutoff: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {
   137	        Self {
   138	            cutoff,
   139	            channel_cutoffs: HashMap::new(),
   140	            package_cutoffs: HashMap::new(),
   141	            include_unknown_timestamp: false,
   142	        }
   143	    }
   144	
   145	    /// Creates a new configuration from a relative duration.
   146	    pub fn from_duration(duration: std::time::Duration) -> Self {
   147	        Self::from_duration_with_now(duration, Utc::now())
   148	    }
   149	
   150	    /// Creates a new configuration from a relative duration and explicit
   151	    /// reference time.
   152	    pub fn from_duration_with_now(duration: std::time::Duration, now: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {
   153	        Self {
   154	            cutoff: Self::cutoff_from_duration(duration, now),
   155	            channel_cutoffs: HashMap::new(),
   156	            package_cutoffs: HashMap::new(),
   157	            include_unknown_timestamp: false,
   158	        }
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    /// Sets the absolute cutoff override for a specific package.
   162	    pub fn with_package_cutoff(mut self, package: PackageName, cutoff: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {
   163	        self.package_cutoffs.insert(package, cutoff);
   164	        self
   165	    }
   166	
   167	    /// Sets the duration override for a specific package.
   168	    pub fn with_package_duration(
   169	        mut self,
   170	        package: PackageName,
   171	        duration: std::time::Duration,
   172	    ) -> Self {
   173	        self.package_cutoffs
   174	            .insert(package, Self::cutoff_from_duration(duration, Utc::now()));
   175	        self
   176	    }
   177	
   178	    /// Sets the duration override for a specific package using an explicit
   179	    /// reference time.
   180	    pub fn with_package_duration_with_now(
   181	        mut self,
   182	        package: PackageName,
   183	        duration: std::time::Duration,
   184	        now: DateTime<Utc>,
   185	    ) -> Self {
   186	        self.package_cutoffs
   187	            .insert(package, Self::cutoff_from_duration(duration, now));
   188	        self
   189	    }
   190	
   191	    /// Sets the duration override for a specific channel.
   192	    pub fn with_channel_duration(
   193	        mut self,
   194	        channel: impl Into<String>,
   195	        duration: std::time::Duration,
   196	    ) -> Self {
   197	        self.channel_cutoffs.insert(
   198	            channel.into(),
   199	            Self::cutoff_from_duration(duration, Utc::now()),
   200	        );
   201	        self
   202	    }
   203	
   204	    /// Sets the duration override for a specific channel using an explicit
   205	    /// reference time.
   206	    pub fn with_channel_duration_with_now(
   207	        mut self,
   208	        channel: impl Into<String>,
   209	        duration: std::time::Duration,
   210	        now: DateTime<Utc>,
   211	    ) -> Self {
   212	        self.channel_cutoffs
   213	            .insert(channel.into(), Self::cutoff_from_duration(duration, now));
   214	        self
   215	    }
   216	
   217	    /// Sets the absolute cutoff override for a specific channel.
   218	    pub fn with_channel_cutoff(
   219	        mut self,
   220	        channel: impl Into<String>,
   221	        cutoff: DateTime<Utc>,
   222	    ) -> Self {
   223	        self.channel_cutoffs.insert(channel.into(), cutoff);
   224	        self
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    /// Sets whether packages without a timestamp should be included.
   228	    ///
   229	    /// Call this to override the constructor default.
   230	    pub fn with_include_unknown_timestamp(mut self, include: bool) -> Self {
   231	        self.include_unknown_timestamp = include;
   232	        self
   233	    }
   234	
   235	    /// Returns whether packages without a timestamp are included.
   236	    pub fn include_unknown_timestamp(&self) -> bool {
   237	        self.include_unknown_timestamp
   238	    }
   239	
   240	    /// Computes the cutoff time for the given package and channel.
   241	    pub fn cutoff_for_package(
   242	        &self,
   243	        package: &PackageName,
   244	        channel: Option<&str>,
   245	    ) -> DateTime<Utc> {
   246	        self.package_cutoffs
   247	            .get(package)
   248	            .copied()
   249	            .or_else(|| channel.and_then(|channel| self.channel_cutoffs.get(channel).copied()))
   250	            .unwrap_or(self.cutoff)
   251	    }
   252	
   253	    /// Returns whether a package should be excluded.
   254	    pub fn is_excluded(
   255	        &self,
   256	        package: &PackageName,
   257	        channel: Option<&str>,
   258	        timestamp: Option<&TimestampMs>,
   259	    ) -> bool {
   260	        match timestamp {
   261	            Some(timestamp) => *timestamp > self.cutoff_for_package(package, channel),
   262	            None => !self.include_unknown_timestamp(),
   263	        }
   264	    }
   265	}
   266	
   267	impl From<DateTime<Utc>> for ExcludeNewer {
   268	    fn from(value: DateTime<Utc>) -> Self {
   269	        Self::from_datetime(value)
   270	    }
   271	}
   272	
   273	impl From<std::time::Duration> for ExcludeNewer {
   274	    fn from(value: std::time::Duration) -> Self {
   275	        Self::from_duration(value)
   276	    }
   277	}
   278	
   279	/// Represents the channel priority option to use during solves.
   280	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
   281	#[cfg_attr(feature = "serde", derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize))]
   282	#[cfg_attr(feature = "serde", serde(rename_all = "kebab-case"))]
   283	pub enum ChannelPriority {
   284	    /// The channel that the package is first found in will be used as the only
   285	    /// channel for that package.
   286	    #[default]
   287	    Strict,
   288	
   289	    // Conda also has "Flexible" as an option, where packages present in multiple channels
   290	    // are only taken from lower-priority channels when this prevents unsatisfiable environment
   291	    // errors, but this would need implementation in the solvers.
   292	    // Flexible,
   293	    /// Packages can be retrieved from any channel as package version takes
   294	    /// precedence.
   295	    Disabled,
   296	}
   297	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
   298	/// Represents a dependency resolution task, to be solved by one of the backends
   299	pub struct SolverTask<TAvailablePackagesIterator> {
   300	    /// An iterator over all available packages
   301	    pub available_packages: TAvailablePackagesIterator,
   302	
   303	    /// Records of packages that are previously selected.
   304	    ///
   305	    /// If the solver encounters multiple variants of a single package
   306	    /// (identified by its name), it will sort the records and select the
   307	    /// best possible version. However, if there exists a locked version it
   308	    /// will prefer that variant instead. This is useful to reduce the number of
   309	    /// packages that are updated when installing new packages.
   310	    ///
   311	    /// Usually you add the currently installed packages or packages from a
   312	    /// lock-file here.
   313	    pub locked_packages: Vec<RepoDataRecord>,
   314	
   315	    /// Records of packages that are previously selected and CANNOT be changed.
   316	    ///
   317	    /// If the solver encounters multiple variants of a single package
   318	    /// (identified by its name), it will sort the records and select the
   319	    /// best possible version. However, if there is a variant available in
   320	    /// the `pinned_packages` field it will always select that version no matter
   321	    /// what even if that means other packages have to be downgraded.
   322	    pub pinned_packages: Vec<RepoDataRecord>,
   323	
   324	    /// Virtual packages considered active
   325	    pub virtual_packages: Vec<GenericVirtualPackage>,
   326	
   327	    /// The specs we want to solve
   328	    pub specs: Vec<MatchSpec>,
   329	
   330	    /// Additional constraints that should be satisfied by the solver.
   331	    /// Packages included in the `constraints` are not necessarily
   332	    /// installed, but they must be satisfied by the solution.
   333	    pub constraints: Vec<MatchSpec>,
   334	
   335	    /// The timeout after which the solver should stop
   336	    pub timeout: Option<std::time::Duration>,
   337	
   338	    /// The channel priority to solve with, either [`ChannelPriority::Strict`]
   339	    /// or [`ChannelPriority::Disabled`]
   340	    pub channel_priority: ChannelPriority,
   341	
   342	    /// Exclude packages newer than the configured cutoff.
   343	    ///
   344	    /// This can be either:
   345	    ///
   346	    /// - a fixed cutoff date, equivalent to the historical `exclude_newer`
   347	    ///   behavior; or
   348	    /// - a relative duration, equivalent to the historical `min_age`
   349	    ///   behavior.
   350	    pub exclude_newer: Option<ExcludeNewer>,
   351	
   352	    /// The solve strategy.
   353	    pub strategy: SolveStrategy,
   354	
   355	    /// Dependency overrides that replace dependencies of matching packages.
   356	    pub dependency_overrides: Vec<(MatchSpec, MatchSpec)>,
   357	}
   358	
   359	impl<'r, I: IntoIterator<Item = &'r RepoDataRecord>> FromIterator<I>
   360	    for SolverTask<Vec<RepoDataIter<I>>>
   361	{
   362	    fn from_iter<T: IntoIterator<Item = I>>(iter: T) -> Self {
   363	        Self {
   364	            available_packages: iter.into_iter().map(|iter| RepoDataIter(iter)).collect(),
   365	            locked_packages: Vec::new(),
   366	            pinned_packages: Vec::new(),
   367	            virtual_packages: Vec::new(),
   368	            specs: Vec::new(),
   369	            constraints: Vec::new(),
   370	            timeout: None,
   371	            channel_priority: ChannelPriority::default(),
   372	            exclude_newer: None,
   373	            strategy: SolveStrategy::default(),
   374	            dependency_overrides: Vec::new(),
   375	        }
   376	    }
   377	}
   378	
   379	/// Represents the strategy to use when solving dependencies
   380	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, Eq, PartialEq)]
   381	#[cfg_attr(feature = "serde", derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize))]
   382	#[cfg_attr(feature = "serde", serde(rename_all = "kebab-case"))]
   383	pub enum SolveStrategy {
   384	    /// Resolve the highest version of each package.
   385	    #[default]
   386	    Highest,
   387	
   388	    /// Resolve the lowest compatible version for each package.
   389	    ///
   390	    /// All candidates with the same version are still ordered the same as
   391	    /// with `Default`. This ensures that the candidate with the highest build
   392	    /// number is used and down-prioritization still works.
   393	    LowestVersion,
   394	
   395	    /// Resolve the lowest compatible version for direct dependencies but the
   396	    /// highest for transitive dependencies. This is similar to `LowestVersion`
   397	    /// but only for direct dependencies.
   398	    LowestVersionDirect,
   399	}
   400	
   401	/// A representation of a collection of [`RepoDataRecord`] usable by a
   402	/// [`SolverImpl`] implementation.
   403	///
   404	/// Some solvers might be able to cache the collection between different runs of
   405	/// the solver which could potentially eliminate some overhead. This trait
   406	/// enables creating a representation of the repodata that is most suitable for
   407	/// a specific backend.
   408	///
   409	/// Some solvers may add additional functionality to their specific
   410	/// implementation that enables caching the repodata to disk in an efficient way
   411	/// (see [`crate::libsolv_c::RepoData`] for an example).
   412	pub trait SolverRepoData<'a>: FromIterator<&'a RepoDataRecord> {}
   413	
   414	/// Defines the ability to convert a type into [`SolverRepoData`].
   415	pub trait IntoRepoData<'a, S: SolverRepoData<'a>> {
   416	    /// Converts this instance into an instance of [`SolverRepoData`] which is
   417	    /// consumable by a specific [`SolverImpl`] implementation.
   418	    fn into(self) -> S;
   419	}
   420	
   421	impl<'a, S: SolverRepoData<'a>> IntoRepoData<'a, S> for &'a Vec<RepoDataRecord> {
   422	    fn into(self) -> S {
   423	        self.iter().collect()
   424	    }
   425	}
   426	
   427	impl<'a, S: SolverRepoData<'a>> IntoRepoData<'a, S> for &'a [RepoDataRecord] {
   428	    fn into(self) -> S {
   429	        self.iter().collect()
   430	    }
   431	}
   432	
   433	impl<'a, S: SolverRepoData<'a>> IntoRepoData<'a, S> for S {
   434	    fn into(self) -> S {
   435	        self
   436	    }
   437	}
   438	
   439	/// A helper struct that implements `IntoRepoData` for anything that can
   440	/// iterate over `RepoDataRecord`s.
   441	pub struct RepoDataIter<T>(pub T);

Now I understand the full picture. Looking at `rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer::cutoff_for_package` (lines 241-251):

```rust
pub fn cutoff_for_package(
    &self,
    package: &PackageName,
    channel: Option<&str>,
) -> DateTime<Utc> {
    self.package_cutoffs
        .get(package)
        .copied()
        .or_else(|| channel.and_then(|channel| self.channel_cutoffs.get(channel).copied()))
        .unwrap_or(self.cutoff)
}
```

The precedence is:
1. Package-level cutoffs (highest priority)
2. Channel-level cutoffs 
3. Global default cutoff

This matches the expected behavior. The issue must be about how channel names are stored/compared.

Looking at `features_ext.rs` line 143:
```rust
.with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
```

Here, `channel.channel` is a `NamedChannelOrUrl`, and `.to_string()` calls the `Display` impl which uses `as_str()`. For a named channel like `"conda-forge"`, this returns `"conda-forge"`. For a URL like `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`, this returns `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` (without trailing slash).

Now, when the solver checks a package, it passes the channel from `RepoDataRecord::channel`. Let me check what format that has...

    37	fn exclude_newer_reason(
    38	    config: &ExcludeNewer,
    39	    package: &PackageName,
    40	    channel: Option<&str>,
    41	    timestamp: Option<&rattler_conda_types::utils::TimestampMs>,
    42	) -> Option<String> {
    43	    let cutoff = config.cutoff_for_package(package, channel);
    44	    match timestamp {
    45	        Some(timestamp) if *timestamp > cutoff => Some(format!(

   360	            // runs.
   361	            let mut ordered_repodata = Vec::with_capacity(repo_data.records.len());
   362	            let mut package_to_type: HashMap<&ArchiveIdentifier, (DistArchiveType, usize, bool)> =
   363	                HashMap::with_capacity(repo_data.records.len());
   364	
   365	            for record in repo_data.records {
   366	                // Determine if this record will be excluded by exclude_newer.
   367	                let excluded = exclude_newer.as_ref().is_some_and(|config| {
   368	                    config.is_excluded(
   369	                        &record.package_record.name,
   370	                        record.channel.as_deref(),
   371	                        record.package_record.timestamp.as_ref(),
   372	                    )
   373	                });
   374	
   375	                let identifier = &record.identifier.identifier;
   376	                let archive_type = record.identifier.archive_type;
   377	                match package_to_type.get_mut(identifier) {
   378	                    None => {
   379	                        let idx = ordered_repodata.len();
   380	                        ordered_repodata.push(record);
   381	                        package_to_type.insert(identifier, (archive_type, idx, excluded));
   382	                    }
   383	                    Some((prev_archive_type, idx, previous_excluded)) => {
   384	                        if *previous_excluded && !excluded {
   385	                            // The previous package would have been excluded by the solver. If the
   386	                            // current record won't be excluded we should always use that.
   387	                            *prev_archive_type = archive_type;
   388	                            ordered_repodata[*idx] = record;
   389	                            *previous_excluded = false;
   390	                        } else if excluded && !*previous_excluded {
   391	                            // The previous package would not have been excluded
   392	                            // by the solver but
   393	                            // this one will, so we'll keep the previous one
   394	                            // regardless of the type.
   395	                        } else {
   396	                            match archive_type.cmp_preference(*prev_archive_type) {
   397	                                Ordering::Greater => {
   398	                                    // A previous package has a worse package "type", we'll use the
   399	                                    // current record instead.
   400	                                    *prev_archive_type = archive_type;
   401	                                    ordered_repodata[*idx] = record;
   402	                                    *previous_excluded = excluded;
   403	                                }
   404	                                Ordering::Less => {
   405	                                    // A previous package that we already stored
   406	                                    // is actually a package of a better
   407	                                    // "type" so we'll just use that instead
   408	                                    // (.conda > .tar.bz)
   409	                                }
   410	                                Ordering::Equal => {
   411	                                    return Err(SolveError::DuplicateRecords(
   412	                                        record.identifier.to_string(),
   413	                                    ));
   414	                                }
   415	                            }
   416	                        }
   417	                    }
   418	                }
   419	            }
   420	
   421	            for record in ordered_repodata {
   422	                let package_name = pool.intern_package_name(&record.package_record.name);
   423	                let solvable_id =
   424	                    pool.intern_solvable(package_name, SolverPackageRecord::Record(record));
   425	
   426	                // Update records with all entries in a single mutable borrow
   427	                let candidates = records.entry(package_name).or_default();
   428	                candidates.candidates.push(solvable_id);
   429	
   430	                if let Some(config) = &exclude_newer {
   431	                    if config.is_excluded(
   432	                        &record.package_record.name,
   433	                        record.channel.as_deref(),
   434	                        record.package_record.timestamp.as_ref(),
   435	                    ) {
   436	                        let reason = pool.intern_string(
   437	                            exclude_newer_reason(
   438	                                config,
   439	                                &record.package_record.name,
   440	                                record.channel.as_deref(),
   441	                                record.package_record.timestamp.as_ref(),
   442	                            )
   443	                            .expect("excluded records must have an exclusion reason"),
   444	                        );
   445	                        candidates.excluded.push((solvable_id, reason));

    29	    /// the package came from. TODO: Refactor this into `Source` which can
    30	    /// be a "name", "channelurl", or "direct url".
    31	    pub channel: Option<String>,
    32	}

Now I see it! The `channel` field in `RepoDataRecord` is an `Option<String>`. The comment says:
> "String representation of the channel where the package comes from. This could be a URL but it could also be a channel name."

Now, the channel name stored in `PrioritizedChannel` is `channel.channel.to_string()` which for a named channel like `conda-forge` gives `"conda-forge"`, and for a URL like `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` gives `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`.

But what does `RepoDataRecord.channel` contain? It's likely the base URL of the channel (something like `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` for the URL-based channel, or `"https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/"` for named channels after resolution).

Wait, the channel matching might not work because the channel name used for the cutoff key (from `channel.channel.to_string()`) might not match the `record.channel` string that the solver passes.

    29	
    30	/// Contains all information that describes the input of a pixi environment.
    31	///
    32	/// Information about binary packages is requested as part of solving this
    33	/// instance.
    34	///
    35	/// When solving a pixi environment, source records are checked out and their
    36	/// metadata is queried. This may involve a recursive pattern of solving if the
    37	/// sources require additional environments to be set up.
    38	///
    39	/// If all the input information is already available and no recursion is
    40	/// desired, use [`SolveCondaEnvironmentSpec`] instead.
    41	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
    42	#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
    43	pub struct PixiEnvironmentSpec {
    44	    pub name: Option<String>,
    45	
    46	    /// The requirements of the environment
    47	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "DependencyMap::is_empty")]
    48	    pub dependencies: DependencyMap<rattler_conda_types::PackageName, PixiSpec>,
    49	
    50	    /// Additional constraints of the environment
    51	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "DependencyMap::is_empty")]
    52	    pub constraints: DependencyMap<rattler_conda_types::PackageName, BinarySpec>,
    53	
    54	    /// Dev sources whose dependencies should be installed without
    55	    /// building the packages themselves.
    56	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "IndexMap::is_empty")]
    57	    pub dev_sources: IndexMap<rattler_conda_types::PackageName, pixi_spec::DevSourceSpec>,
    58	
    59	    /// The records of the packages that are currently already installed. These
    60	    /// are used as hints to reduce the difference between individual solves.
    61	    #[serde(skip)]
    62	    pub installed: Vec<PixiRecord>,
    63	
    64	    /// The environment that we are solving for
    65	    pub build_environment: BuildEnvironment,
    66	
    67	    /// The channels to use for solving
    68	    pub channels: Vec<ChannelUrl>,
    69	
    70	    /// The strategy to use for solving
    71	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "crate::is_default")]
    72	    pub strategy: SolveStrategy,
    73	
    74	    /// The priority of channels to use for solving
    75	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "crate::is_default")]
    76	    pub channel_priority: ChannelPriority,
    77	
    78	    /// Exclude packages newer than the configured default and per-channel cutoffs.
    79	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    80	    pub exclude_newer: Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>,
    81	
    82	    /// The channel configuration to use for this environment.
    83	    pub channel_config: ChannelConfig,
    84	
    85	    /// Build variants to use during the solve
    86	    pub variant_configuration: Option<BTreeMap<String, Vec<VariantValue>>>,
    87	
    88	    /// Variant file paths to use during the solve
    89	    pub variant_files: Option<Vec<PathBuf>>,
    90	
    91	    /// The protocols that are enabled for source packages
    92	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "crate::is_default")]
    93	    pub enabled_protocols: EnabledProtocols,
    94	
    95	    /// Optional override for a specific packages: use this pinned
    96	    /// source for checkout and as the `package_build_source` instead
    97	    /// of recomputing the pinned location.
    98	    #[serde(skip)]
    99	    pub preferred_build_source:
   100	        BTreeMap<rattler_conda_types::PackageName, pixi_record::PinnedSourceSpec>,

    91	impl Default for SolveCondaEnvironmentSpec {
    92	    fn default() -> Self {
    93	        Self {
    94	            name: None,
    95	            source_specs: DependencyMap::default(),
    96	            binary_specs: DependencyMap::default(),
    97	            constraints: DependencyMap::default(),
    98	            dev_source_records: vec![],
    99	            source_repodata: vec![],
   100	            binary_repodata: vec![],
   101	            installed: vec![],
   102	            platform: Platform::current(),
   103	            channels: vec![],
   104	            virtual_packages: vec![],
   105	            strategy: SolveStrategy::default(),
   106	            channel_priority: ChannelPriority::default(),
   107	            exclude_newer: None,
   108	            channel_config: ChannelConfig::default_with_root_dir(PathBuf::from(".")),
   109	        }
   110	    }
   111	}
   112	
   113	impl SolveCondaEnvironmentSpec {
   114	    /// Solves this environment
   115	    pub async fn solve(
   116	        self,
   117	    ) -> Result<Vec<PixiRecord>, CommandDispatcherError<SolveCondaEnvironmentError>> {
   118	        // Solving is a CPU-intensive task, we spawn this on a background task to allow
   119	        // for more concurrency.
   120	        let solve_result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
   121	            let exclude_newer = self.exclude_newer.clone().map(Into::into);
   122	
   123	            // Determine for which records we have source records because those records should only
   124	            //  be installed as source records.
   125	            let package_names_from_source = self
   126	                .source_repodata
   127	                .iter()
   128	                .flat_map(|metadata| &metadata.records)
   129	                .map(|metadata| &metadata.package_record.name)
   130	                .dedup()
   131	                .collect::<HashSet<_>>();
   132	
   133	            // Filter all installed packages
   134	            let installed = self
   135	                .installed
   136	                .into_iter()
   137	                // Only lock binary records
   138	                .filter_map(|record| record.into_binary())
   139	                // Filter any record we want as a source record
   140	                .filter(|record| !package_names_from_source.contains(&record.package_record.name))

   281	            let mut solvable_records = Vec::with_capacity(self.binary_repodata.len() + 1);
   282	            solvable_records.push(
   283	                url_to_source_package
   284	                    .values()
   285	                    .map(|(_, record)| record)
   286	                    .chain(url_to_dev_source.values().map(|(_, record)| record))

   295	                specs: source_match_specs
   296	                    .into_iter()
   297	                    .chain(binary_match_specs)
   298	                    .chain(dev_source_match_specs)

   313	                solver_result
   314	                    .records
   315	                    .into_iter()
   316	                    .filter_map(|record| {
   317	                        if let Some(source_record) = url_to_source_package.remove(&record.url) {

   319	                            // instead of the binary record.

   323	                            return None;
   324	                        }

Now I see the flow:
1. `ResolvedExcludeNewer` is converted to `rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer` via `Into::into()` (line 121)
2. This is passed to the solver task (line 303)
3. The solver uses `record.channel.as_deref()` to get the channel string and matches it against `self.channel_cutoffs`

Now the key question: what does `record.channel` look like vs what we store in `channel_cutoffs`?

In `features_ext.rs`:
```rust
.with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt)
```

This uses `NamedChannelOrUrl::to_string()` which returns:
- For named channels like `conda-forge`: `"conda-forge"`
- For URLs like `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`: `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`

But in the solver, `record.channel` is likely set to the **base URL** of the channel (after resolution). For example:
- For `conda-forge`: might be `"https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/"` (the resolved URL)
- For `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`: might be `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` (similar but might have trailing slash differences)

Let me check where `record.channel` is set in the repodata fetching

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /usr/local/cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/rattler_repodata_gateway-0.27.4/src/gateway/remote_subdir/tokio.rs:
     1	use std::{
     2	    path::{Path, PathBuf},
     3	    sync::Arc,
     4	};
     5	
     6	use crate::{
     7	    fetch::{fetch_repo_data, FetchRepoDataError, FetchRepoDataOptions, Variant},
     8	    gateway::{
     9	        error::SubdirNotFoundError, local_subdir::LocalSubdirClient, GatewayError, SourceConfig,
    10	    },
    11	    Reporter,
    12	};
    13	use rattler_conda_types::{Channel, Platform};
    14	use rattler_networking::LazyClient;
    15	
    16	pub struct RemoteSubdirClient {
    17	    pub(super) sparse: LocalSubdirClient,
    18	}
    19	
    20	impl RemoteSubdirClient {
    21	    pub async fn new(
    22	        channel: Channel,
    23	        platform: Platform,
    24	        client: LazyClient,
    25	        cache_dir: PathBuf,
    26	        source_config: SourceConfig,
    27	        reporter: Option<Arc<dyn Reporter>>,
    28	    ) -> Result<Self, GatewayError> {
    29	        let subdir_url = channel.platform_url(platform);
    30	
    31	        // Fetch the repodata from the remote server
    32	        let repodata = fetch_repo_data(
    33	            subdir_url,
    34	            client,
    35	            cache_dir,
    36	            FetchRepoDataOptions {
    37	                cache_action: source_config.cache_action,
    38	                zstd_enabled: source_config.zstd_enabled,
    39	                bz2_enabled: source_config.bz2_enabled,
    40	                ..FetchRepoDataOptions::default()
    41	            },
    42	            reporter,
    43	        )
    44	        .await
    45	        .map_err(|e| match e {
    46	            FetchRepoDataError::NotFound(e) => {
    47	                GatewayError::SubdirNotFoundError(Box::new(SubdirNotFoundError {
    48	                    channel: channel.clone(),
    49	                    subdir: platform.to_string(),
    50	                    source: e.into(),
    51	                }))
    52	            }
    53	            e => GatewayError::FetchRepoDataError(e),
    54	        })?;
    55	
    56	        // Create a new sparse repodata client that can be used to read records from the
    57	        // repodata.
    58	        let sparse = simple_spawn_blocking::tokio::run_blocking_task(move || {
    59	            LocalSubdirClient::from_file(
    60	                &repodata.repo_data_json_path,
    61	                channel.clone(),
    62	                platform.as_str(),
    63	            )
    64	        })
    65	        .await?;
    66	
    67	        Ok(Self { sparse })
    68	    }
    69	
    70	    /// Clears the on-disk cache for the given channel and platform.
    71	    ///
    72	    /// This removes all cached repodata files (JSON and info files) for
    73	    /// the specified channel and platform combination. The lock file is
    74	    /// retained to avoid the ABA problem with concurrent processes.
    75	    ///
    76	    /// If the cache directory or lock file doesn't exist, this is a no-op
    77	    /// since there's nothing to clear.
    78	    pub fn clear_cache(
    79	        cache_dir: &Path,
    80	        channel: &Channel,
    81	        platform: Platform,
    82	    ) -> Result<(), std::io::Error> {
    83	        let subdir_url = channel.platform_url(platform);
    84	        let cache_key = crate::utils::url_to_cache_filename(
    85	            &subdir_url
    86	                .join(Variant::default().file_name())
    87	                .expect("valid filename"),
    88	        );
    89	
    90	        // Acquire a lock before modifying the cache files.
    91	        // If the lock file doesn't exist (e.g., parent directory doesn't exist),
    92	        // then there's no cache to clear, so we can return early.
    93	        let lock_path = cache_dir.join(format!("{cache_key}.lock"));
    94	        let _lock = match crate::utils::LockedFile::open_rw(&lock_path, "repodata cache clear") {
    95	            Ok(lock) => lock,
    96	            Err(_) => {
    97	                // Lock file doesn't exist or can't be created - no cache to clear
    98	                return Ok(());
    99	            }
   100	        };
   101	
   102	        // Remove the cached repodata files (but NOT the lock file)
   103	        let json_path = cache_dir.join(format!("{cache_key}.json"));
   104	        let info_path = cache_dir.join(format!("{cache_key}.info.json"));
   105	
   106	        for path in [json_path, info_path] {
   107	            if path.exists() {
   108	                fs_err::remove_file(&path)?;
   109	                tracing::debug!("deleted repodata cache file: {:?}", path);
   110	            }
   111	        }
   112	
   113	        Ok(())
   114	    }
   115	}
   116

    36	    Default,
    37	    Debug,
    38	    Clone,
    39	    Copy,
