    47	#[strum(serialize_all = "kebab-case")]
    48	pub enum PackageFormatSelection {
    49	    /// Only the tar.bz2 packages are used
    50	    OnlyTarBz2,
    51	
    52	    /// Only the conda packages are used
    53	    OnlyConda,
    54	
    55	    /// Both .tar.bz2 and .conda packages are used, but if a .conda exists that
    56	    /// represents the same content as a .tar.bz2, the .conda package is
    57	    /// selected and the .tar.bz2 is discarded.
    58	    #[default]
    59	    PreferConda,
    60	
    61	    /// .tar.bz2, .conda and .whl packages are used, but if a .conda exists that
    62	    /// represents the same content as a .tar.bz2 or .whl, the .conda package is
    63	    /// selected and the .tar.bz2 is discarded.
    64	    PreferCondaWithWhl,
    65	
    66	    /// Both .tar.bz2 and .conda packages are used
    67	    Both,
    68	}
    69	
    70	/// A struct to enable loading records from a `repodata.json` file on demand.
    71	/// Since most of the time you don't need all the records from the
    72	/// `repodata.json` this can help provide some significant speedups.
    73	pub struct SparseRepoData {
    74	    /// Data structure that holds an index into the the records stored in a repo
    75	    /// data.
    76	    inner: SparseRepoDataInner,
    77	
    78	    /// The channel from which this data was downloaded.
    79	    channel: Channel,
    80	
    81	    /// The subdirectory from where the repodata is downloaded
    82	    subdir: String,
    83	
    84	    /// A function that can be used to patch the package record after it has
    85	    /// been parsed. This is mainly used to add `pip` to `python` if desired
    86	    patch_record_fn: Option<fn(&mut PackageRecord)>,
    87	}
    88	
    89	enum SparseRepoDataInner {
    90	    /// The repo data is stored as a memory mapped file
    91	    #[cfg(any(unix, windows))]
    92	    Memmapped(MemmappedSparseRepoDataInner),
    93	    /// The repo data is stored as `Bytes`
    94	    Bytes(BytesSparseRepoDataInner),
    95	}
    96	
    97	impl SparseRepoDataInner {
    98	    fn borrow_repo_data(&self) -> &LazyRepoData<'_> {
    99	        match self {
   100	            #[cfg(any(unix, windows))]
   101	            SparseRepoDataInner::Memmapped(inner) => inner.borrow_dependent(),
   102	            SparseRepoDataInner::Bytes(inner) => inner.borrow_dependent(),
   103	        }
   104	    }
   105	}
   106	
   107	// A struct that holds a memory map of a `repodata.json` file and also a
   108	// self-referential field which indexes the data in the memory map with a
   109	// sparsely parsed json struct. See [`LazyRepoData`].
   110	#[cfg(any(unix, windows))]
   111	self_cell::self_cell!(
   112	    struct MemmappedSparseRepoDataInner {
   113	        // Memory map of the `repodata.json` file
   114	        owner: memmap2::Mmap,
   115	
   116	        // Sparsely parsed json content of the memory map. This data struct holds
   117	        // references into the memory map so we have to use ouroboros to make
   118	        // this legal.
   119	        #[covariant]
   120	        dependent: LazyRepoData,
   121	    }
   122	);
   123	
   124	// A struct that holds a reference to the bytes of a `repodata.json` file and
   125	// also a self-referential field which indexes the data in the `bytes` with a
   126	// sparsely parsed json struct. See [`LazyRepoData`].
   127	self_cell::self_cell!(
   128	    struct BytesSparseRepoDataInner {
   129	        // Bytes of the `repodata.json` file
   130	        owner: Bytes,
   131	
   132	        // Sparsely parsed json content of the file's bytes. This data struct holds
   133	        // references into the bytes so we have to use ouroboros to make this
   134	        // legal.
   135	        #[covariant]
   136	        dependent: LazyRepoData,
   137	    }
   138	);
   139	
   140	impl SparseRepoData {
   141	    /// Construct an instance of self from a file on disk and a [`Channel`].
   142	    ///
   143	    /// The `patch_function` can be used to patch the package record after it
   144	    /// has been parsed (e.g. to add `pip` to `python`).
   145	    ///
   146	    /// On Windows the file is opened with `FILE_SHARE_DELETE` so that another
   147	    /// process/thread can rename or delete the file while it is still mapped.
   148	    #[cfg(any(unix, windows))]
   149	    pub fn from_file(
   150	        channel: Channel,
   151	        subdir: impl Into<String>,
   152	        path: impl AsRef<Path>,
   153	        patch_function: Option<fn(&mut PackageRecord)>,
   154	    ) -> Result<Self, io::Error> {
   155	        #[cfg(windows)]
   156	        let file = {
   157	            use std::os::windows::fs::OpenOptionsExt;
   158	            const SHARE_ALL: u32 = 0x01 | 0x02 | 0x04; // FILE_SHARE_READ | WRITE | DELETE
   159	            std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
   160	                .read(true)
   161	                .share_mode(SHARE_ALL)
   162	                .open(path.as_ref())?
   163	        };
   164	        #[cfg(not(windows))]
   165	        let file = std::fs::File::from(fs_err::File::open(path.as_ref())?);
   166	
   167	        let memory_map = unsafe { memmap2::Mmap::map(&file) }?;
   168	        Ok(SparseRepoData {
   169	            inner: SparseRepoDataInner::Memmapped(MemmappedSparseRepoDataInner::try_new(
   170	                memory_map,
   171	                |memory_map| serde_json::from_slice(memory_map.as_ref()),
   172	            )?),
   173	            subdir: subdir.into(),
   174	            channel,
   175	            patch_record_fn: patch_function,
   176	        })
   177	    }
   178	
   179	    /// Construct an instance of self from a file on disk and a [`Channel`].
   180	    ///
   181	    /// The `patch_function` can be used to patch the package record after it
   182	    /// has been parsed (e.g. to add `pip` to `python`).
   183	    #[cfg(not(any(windows, unix)))]
   184	    pub fn from_file(
   185	        channel: Channel,
   186	        subdir: impl Into<String>,
   187	        path: impl AsRef<Path>,
   188	        patch_function: Option<fn(&mut PackageRecord)>,
   189	    ) -> Result<Self, io::Error> {
   190	        let bytes = fs_err::read(path)?;
   191	        Ok(Self::from_bytes(
   192	            channel,
   193	            subdir,
   194	            bytes.into(),
   195	            patch_function,
   196	        )?)
   197	    }
   198	
   199	    /// Construct an instance of self from a bytes and a [`Channel`].
   200	    ///
   201	    /// The `patch_function` can be used to patch the package record after it
   202	    /// has been parsed (e.g. to add `pip` to `python`).
   203	    pub fn from_bytes(
   204	        channel: Channel,
   205	        subdir: impl Into<String>,
   206	        bytes: Bytes,
   207	        patch_function: Option<fn(&mut PackageRecord)>,
   208	    ) -> Result<Self, serde_json::Error> {
   209	        Ok(Self {
   210	            inner: SparseRepoDataInner::Bytes(BytesSparseRepoDataInner::try_new(bytes, |bytes| {
   211	                serde_json::from_slice(bytes)
   212	            })?),
   213	            channel,
   214	            subdir: subdir.into(),
   215	            patch_record_fn: patch_function,
   216	        })
   217	    }
   218	
   219	    /// Returns an iterator over all package names in this repodata file.
   220	    ///
   221	    /// This works by iterating over all elements in the `packages` and
   222	    /// `conda_packages` fields of the repodata and returning the unique
   223	    /// package names.
   224	    pub fn package_names(
   225	        &self,
   226	        package_format_selection: PackageFormatSelection,
   227	    ) -> impl Iterator<Item = &'_ str> {
   228	        fn select_package_name<'i>((filename, _): &(PackageFilename<'i>, &'i RawValue)) -> &'i str {
   229	            filename.package
   230	        }
   231	
   232	        let repo_data = self.inner.borrow_repo_data();
   233	        let tar_bz2_packages = repo_data.packages.iter().map(select_package_name);
   234	        let conda_packages = repo_data.conda_packages.iter().map(select_package_name);
   235	        let v3_tar = repo_data
   236	            .experimental_v3
   237	            .tar_bz2
   238	            .iter()
   239	            .map(select_package_name);
   240	        let v3_conda = repo_data
   241	            .experimental_v3
   242	            .conda
   243	            .iter()
   244	            .map(select_package_name);
   245	        let v3_whl = repo_data
   246	            .experimental_v3
   247	            .whl
   248	            .iter()
   249	            .map(select_package_name);
   250	
   251	        match package_format_selection {
   252	            PackageFormatSelection::Both | PackageFormatSelection::PreferConda => {
   253	                itertools::Either::Left(itertools::Either::Left(
   254	                    tar_bz2_packages
   255	                        .merge(v3_tar)
   256	                        .merge(conda_packages.merge(v3_conda))
   257	                        .dedup(),
   258	                ))
   259	            }
   260	            PackageFormatSelection::PreferCondaWithWhl => {
   261	                itertools::Either::Left(itertools::Either::Right(
   262	                    tar_bz2_packages
   263	                        .merge(v3_tar)
   264	                        .merge(v3_whl)
   265	                        .merge(conda_packages.merge(v3_conda))
   266	                        .dedup(),
   267	                ))
   268	            }
   269	            PackageFormatSelection::OnlyTarBz2 => itertools::Either::Right(
   270	                itertools::Either::Left(tar_bz2_packages.merge(v3_tar).dedup()),
   271	            ),
   272	            PackageFormatSelection::OnlyConda => itertools::Either::Right(
   273	                itertools::Either::Right(conda_packages.merge(v3_conda).dedup()),
   274	            ),
   275	        }
   276	    }
   277	
   278	    /// Returns the number of records in this instance.
   279	    pub fn record_count(&self, package_format_selection: PackageFormatSelection) -> usize {
   280	        let repo_data = self.inner.borrow_repo_data();
   281	        match package_format_selection {
   282	            PackageFormatSelection::PreferConda | PackageFormatSelection::PreferCondaWithWhl => {
   283	                let tar_bz2_packages = repo_data.packages.iter().map(|(filename, _)| {
   284	                    filename
   285	                        .filename
   286	                        .strip_suffix(CondaArchiveType::TarBz2.extension())
   287	                        .unwrap_or(filename.filename)
   288	                });
   289	                let v3_tar = repo_data
   290	                    .experimental_v3
   291	                    .tar_bz2
   292	                    .iter()
   293	                    .map(|(filename, _)| filename.filename);
   294	                let conda_packages = repo_data.conda_packages.iter().map(|(filename, _)| {
   295	                    filename
   296	                        .filename
   297	                        .strip_suffix(CondaArchiveType::Conda.extension())
   298	                        .unwrap_or(filename.filename)
   299	                });
   300	                let v3_conda = repo_data
   301	                    .experimental_v3
   302	                    .conda
   303	                    .iter()
   304	                    .map(|(filename, _)| filename.filename);
   305	
   306	                if package_format_selection == PackageFormatSelection::PreferCondaWithWhl {
   307	                    let v3_whl = repo_data
   308	                        .experimental_v3
   309	                        .whl
   310	                        .iter()
   311	                        .map(|(filename, _)| filename.filename);
   312	                    conda_packages
   313	                        .merge(v3_conda)
   314	                        .merge(tar_bz2_packages.merge(v3_tar))
   315	                        .merge(v3_whl)
   316	                        .dedup()
   317	                        .count()
   318	                } else {
   319	                    conda_packages
   320	                        .merge(v3_conda)
   321	                        .merge(tar_bz2_packages.merge(v3_tar))
   322	                        .dedup()
   323	                        .count()
   324	                }
   325	            }
   326	            PackageFormatSelection::Both => {
   327	                repo_data.packages.len()
   328	                    + repo_data.conda_packages.len()
   329	                    + repo_data.experimental_v3.tar_bz2.len()
   330	                    + repo_data.experimental_v3.conda.len()
   331	            }
   332	            PackageFormatSelection::OnlyTarBz2 => {
   333	                repo_data.packages.len() + repo_data.experimental_v3.tar_bz2.len()
   334	            }
   335	            PackageFormatSelection::OnlyConda => {
   336	                repo_data.conda_packages.len() + repo_data.experimental_v3.conda.len()
   337	            }
   338	        }
   339	    }
   340	
   341	    /// Returns all the records that matches any of the specified match spec.
   342	    pub fn load_matching_records(
   343	        &self,
   344	        spec: impl IntoIterator<Item = impl Borrow<MatchSpec>>,
   345	        variant_consolidation: PackageFormatSelection,
   346	    ) -> io::Result<Vec<RepoDataRecord>> {
   347	        let mut result = Vec::new();
   348	        let repo_data = self.inner.borrow_repo_data();
   349	        let base_url = repo_data.info.as_ref().and_then(|i| i.base_url.as_deref());
   350	        for (package_name, specs) in &spec.into_iter().chunk_by(|spec| spec.borrow().name.clone()) {
   351	            let grouped_specs = specs.into_iter().collect::<Vec<_>>();
   352	            // TODO: support glob/regex package names
   353	            let mut parsed_records = parse_records(
   354	                package_name.as_exact(),
   355	                &repo_data.packages,
   356	                &repo_data.conda_packages,
   357	                &repo_data.experimental_v3,
   358	                variant_consolidation,
   359	                base_url,
   360	                &self.channel,
   361	                &self.subdir,
   362	                self.patch_record_fn,
   363	                |record| {
   364	                    grouped_specs
   365	                        .iter()
   366	                        .any(|spec| spec.borrow().matches(&record.package_record))
   367	                },
   368	            )?;
   369	            result.append(&mut parsed_records);
   370	        }
   371	
   372	        Ok(result)
   373	    }
   374	
   375	    /// Returns all the records for the specified package name.
   376	    pub fn load_records(
   377	        &self,
   378	        package_name: &PackageName,
   379	        variant_consolidation: PackageFormatSelection,
   380	    ) -> io::Result<Vec<RepoDataRecord>> {
   381	        let repo_data = self.inner.borrow_repo_data();
   382	        let base_url = repo_data.info.as_ref().and_then(|i| i.base_url.as_deref());
   383	        parse_records(
   384	            Some(package_name),
   385	            &repo_data.packages,
   386	            &repo_data.conda_packages,
   387	            &repo_data.experimental_v3,
   388	            variant_consolidation,
   389	            base_url,
   390	            &self.channel,
   391	            &self.subdir,
   392	            self.patch_record_fn,
   393	            |_| true, // Dont filter anything out
   394	        )
   395	    }
   396	
   397	    /// Returns all the records for the specified package format(s).
   398	    pub fn load_all_records(
   399	        &self,

   402	        let repo_data = self.inner.borrow_repo_data();

   410	            base_url,
   411	            &self.channel,
   412	            &self.subdir,
   413	            self.patch_record_fn,
   414	            |_| true,
   415	        )
   416	    }

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /usr/local/cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/rattler_repodata_gateway-0.27.4/src/sparse/mod.rs:
   602	fn parse_records<'i, F: Fn(&RepoDataRecord) -> bool>(
   603	    package_name: Option<&PackageName>,
   604	    tar_bz2_packages: &[(PackageFilename<'i>, &'i RawValue)],
   605	    conda_packages: &[(PackageFilename<'i>, &'i RawValue)],
   606	    v3: &LazyV3Packages<'i>,
   607	    variant_consolidation: PackageFormatSelection,
   608	    base_url: Option<&str>,
   609	    channel: &Channel,
   610	    subdir: &str,
   611	    patch_function: Option<fn(&mut PackageRecord)>,
   612	    filter_function: F,
   613	) -> io::Result<Vec<RepoDataRecord>> {
   614	    match variant_consolidation {
   615	        PackageFormatSelection::PreferConda => {
   616	            let tar_bz2 = add_stripped_filename(
   617	                find_package_in_slice(tar_bz2_packages, package_name, RecordKind::CondaOrTarBz2),
   618	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::TarBz2),
   619	            );
   620	            let v3_tar = add_stripped_filename(
   621	                find_package_in_slice(&v3.tar_bz2, package_name, RecordKind::V3TarBz2),
   622	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::TarBz2),
   623	            );
   624	            let all_tar = tar_bz2.merge_by(v3_tar, |(_, _, _, a), (_, _, _, b)| a <= b);
   625	
   626	            let conda = add_stripped_filename(
   627	                find_package_in_slice(conda_packages, package_name, RecordKind::CondaOrTarBz2),
   628	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::Conda),
   629	            );
   630	            let v3_conda_iter = add_stripped_filename(
   631	                find_package_in_slice(&v3.conda, package_name, RecordKind::V3Conda),
   632	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::Conda),
   633	            );
   634	            let all_conda = conda.merge_by(v3_conda_iter, |(_, _, _, a), (_, _, _, b)| a <= b);
   635	
   636	            let deduplicated_packages = all_conda
   637	                .merge_by(all_tar, |(_, _, _, left), (_, _, _, right)| left <= right)
   638	                .dedup_by(|(_, _, _, left), (_, _, _, right)| left == right)
   639	                .map(|(filename, raw_json, record_kind, _)| (filename, raw_json, record_kind));
   640	            parse_records_raw(
   641	                deduplicated_packages,
   642	                base_url,
   643	                channel,
   644	                subdir,
   645	                patch_function,
   646	                filter_function,
   647	            )
   648	        }
   649	        PackageFormatSelection::PreferCondaWithWhl => {
   650	            let tar_bz2 = add_stripped_filename(
   651	                find_package_in_slice(tar_bz2_packages, package_name, RecordKind::CondaOrTarBz2),
   652	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::TarBz2),
   653	            );
   654	            let v3_tar = add_stripped_filename(
   655	                find_package_in_slice(&v3.tar_bz2, package_name, RecordKind::V3TarBz2),
   656	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::TarBz2),
   657	            );
   658	            let all_tar = tar_bz2.merge_by(v3_tar, |(_, _, _, a), (_, _, _, b)| a <= b);
   659	
   660	            let whl = add_stripped_filename(
   661	                find_package_in_slice(&v3.whl, package_name, RecordKind::V3Whl),
   662	                DistArchiveType::from(WheelArchiveType::Whl),
   663	            );
   664	
   665	            let conda = add_stripped_filename(
   666	                find_package_in_slice(conda_packages, package_name, RecordKind::CondaOrTarBz2),
   667	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::Conda),
   668	            );
   669	            let v3_conda_iter = add_stripped_filename(
   670	                find_package_in_slice(&v3.conda, package_name, RecordKind::V3Conda),
   671	                DistArchiveType::from(CondaArchiveType::Conda),
   672	            );
   673	            let all_conda = conda.merge_by(v3_conda_iter, |(_, _, _, a), (_, _, _, b)| a <= b);
   674	
   675	            let deduplicated_packages = all_conda
   676	                .merge_by(whl, |(_, _, _, left), (_, _, _, right)| left <= right)
   677	                .merge_by(all_tar, |(_, _, _, left), (_, _, _, right)| left <= right)
   678	                .dedup_by(|(_, _, _, left), (_, _, _, right)| left == right)
   679	                .map(|(filename, raw_json, kind, _)| (filename, raw_json, kind));
   680	            parse_records_raw(
   681	                deduplicated_packages,
   682	                base_url,
   683	                channel,
   684	                subdir,
   685	                patch_function,
   686	                filter_function,
   687	            )
   688	        }
   689	        PackageFormatSelection::Both => {
   690	            let tar_bz2 =
   691	                find_package_in_slice(tar_bz2_packages, package_name, RecordKind::CondaOrTarBz2);
   692	            let conda =
   693	                find_package_in_slice(conda_packages, package_name, RecordKind::CondaOrTarBz2);
   694	            let v3_tar = find_package_in_slice(&v3.tar_bz2, package_name, RecordKind::V3TarBz2);
   695	            let v3_conda_iter = find_package_in_slice(&v3.conda, package_name, RecordKind::V3Conda);
   696	            parse_records_raw(
   697	                tar_bz2.chain(conda).chain(v3_tar).chain(v3_conda_iter),
   698	                base_url,
   699	                channel,
   700	                subdir,

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /usr/local/cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/rattler_repodata_gateway-0.27.4/src/sparse/mod.rs:
   898	fn parse_records_raw<'i, F: Fn(&RepoDataRecord) -> bool>(
   899	    packages: impl Iterator<Item = (PackageFilename<'i>, &'i RawValue, RecordKind)>,
   900	    base_url: Option<&str>,
   901	    channel: &Channel,
   902	    subdir: &str,
   903	    patch_function: Option<fn(&mut PackageRecord)>,
   904	    filter_function: F,
   905	) -> io::Result<Vec<RepoDataRecord>> {
   906	    let channel_name = channel.base_url.url().clone().redact().to_string();
   907	    packages
   908	        .map(move |record| {
   909	            parse_record_raw(
   910	                record,
   911	                base_url,
   912	                channel,
   913	                Some(channel_name.clone()),
   914	                subdir,
   915	                patch_function,
   916	            )
   917	        })
   918	        .filter_ok(filter_function)
   919	        .collect()
   920	}
   921	
   922	/// A helper function that immediately loads the records for the given packages
   923	/// (and their dependencies). Records for the specified packages are loaded from
   924	/// the repodata files. The `patch_record_fn` is applied to each record after it
   925	/// has been parsed and can mutate the record after it has been loaded.
   926	#[cfg(any(unix, windows))]
   927	pub async fn load_repo_data_recursively(
   928	    repo_data_paths: impl IntoIterator<Item = (Channel, impl Into<String>, impl AsRef<Path>)>,
   929	    package_names: impl IntoIterator<Item = PackageName>,
   930	    patch_function: Option<fn(&mut PackageRecord)>,
   931	    variant_consolidation: PackageFormatSelection,
   932	) -> Result<Vec<Vec<RepoDataRecord>>, io::Error> {
   933	    use futures::{StreamExt, TryFutureExt, TryStreamExt};
   934	
   935	    // Open the different files and memory map them to get access to their bytes. Do
   936	    // this in parallel.
   937	    let lazy_repo_data = futures::stream::iter(repo_data_paths)
   938	        .map(|(channel, subdir, path)| {
   939	            let path = path.as_ref().to_path_buf();
   940	            let subdir = subdir.into();
   941	            tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
   942	                SparseRepoData::from_file(channel, subdir, path, patch_function)
   943	            })
   944	            .unwrap_or_else(|r| match r.try_into_panic() {
   945	                Ok(panic) => std::panic::resume_unwind(panic),
   946	                Err(err) => Err(io::Error::other(err.to_string())),
   947	            })
   948	        })
   949	        .buffered(50)
   950	        .try_collect::<Vec<_>>()
   951	        .await?;
   952	
   953	    SparseRepoData::load_records_recursive(
   954	        &lazy_repo_data,
   955	        package_names,
   956	        patch_function,
   957	        variant_consolidation,
   958	    )
   959	}
   960	
   961	fn deserialize_filename_and_raw_record<'d, D: Deserializer<'d>>(
   962	    deserializer: D,
   963	) -> Result<Vec<(PackageFilename<'d>, &'d RawValue)>, D::Error> {
   964	    #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)]
   965	    struct MapVisitor<I, K, V>(PhantomData<fn() -> (I, K, V)>);
   966	
   967	    impl<'de, I, K, V> Visitor<'de> for MapVisitor<I, K, V>
   968	    where
   969	        I: FromIterator<(K, V)>,
   970	        K: Deserialize<'de>,
   971	        V: Deserialize<'de>,
   972	    {
   973	        type Value = I;
   974	
   975	        fn expecting(&self, formatter: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   976	            formatter.write_str("a map")
   977	        }
   978	
   979	        fn visit_map<A>(self, map: A) -> Result<Self::Value, A::Error>
   980	        where
   981	            A: MapAccess<'de>,
   982	        {
   983	            let iter = MapIter(map, PhantomData);
   984	            iter.collect()
   985	        }
   986	    }
   987	
   988	    struct MapIter<'de, A, K, V>(A, PhantomData<(&'de (), A, K, V)>);
   989	
   990	    impl<'de, A, K, V> Iterator for MapIter<'de, A, K, V>
   991	    where
   992	        A: MapAccess<'de>,
   993	        K: Deserialize<'de>,
   994	        V: Deserialize<'de>,
   995	    {
   996	        type Item = Result<(K, V), A::Error>;
   997	
   998	        fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> {
   999	            match self.0.next_entry() {
  1000	                Ok(Some(x)) => Some(Ok(x)),

Now I can see the key line 906:
```rust
let channel_name = channel.base_url.url().clone().redact().to_string();
```

The `channel` field on `RepoDataRecord` is set to the base URL of the channel (the redacted URL string). This is the full URL like `"https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/"` (for named channels) or `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` (for URL channels).

But in `features_ext.rs`, the channel key is set using:
```rust
.with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt)
```

Where `channel.channel` is a `NamedChannelOrUrl`. For a named channel like `conda-forge`, `to_string()` returns `"conda-forge"`. But the record's channel will be something like `"https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/"` — these don't match!

For a URL channel like `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`, `to_string()` returns `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`. The record's channel might be `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` or similar. But the match might still work because the record's channel is derived from the same URL.

Wait, but the tests seem to pass. Let me look at the test more carefully:

```rust
config.cutoff_for_package(&package, Some("bioconda"))
```

The test passes `"bioconda"` (just the name) to `cutoff_for_package`. And in the solver, `record.channel` is the base URL. But the test is directly calling `cutoff_for_package` with the channel name. So in the actual solver path, the lookup key needs to match.

   191	pub struct Channel {
   192	    /// The platforms supported by this channel, or None if no explicit
   193	    /// platforms have been specified.
   194	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   195	    pub platforms: Option<Vec<Platform>>,
   196	
   197	    /// Base URL of the channel, everything is relative to this url.
   198	    pub base_url: ChannelUrl,
   199	
   200	    /// The name of the channel
   201	    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   202	    pub name: Option<String>,
   203	}
   204	
   205	impl Channel {
   206	    /// Parses a [`Channel`] from a string and a channel configuration.
   207	    pub fn from_str(
   208	        str: impl AsRef<str>,
   209	        config: &ChannelConfig,
   210	    ) -> Result<Self, ParseChannelError> {
   211	        let str = str.as_ref();
   212	        let (platforms, channel) = parse_platforms(str)?;
   213	
   214	        let channel = if parse_scheme(channel).is_some() {
   215	            let url = Url::parse(channel)?;
   216	            let name = config.strip_channel_alias(&url);
   217	            let base_channel = Channel::from_url(url);
   218	
   219	            Channel {
   220	                name: name.or(base_channel.name),
   221	                platforms,
   222	                ..base_channel
   223	            }
   224	        } else if is_path(channel) {
   225	            #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
   226	            return Err(ParseChannelError::InvalidPath(channel.to_string()));
   227	
   228	            #[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
   229	            {
   230	                let absolute_path = absolute_path(channel, &config.root_dir)?;
   231	                let url = directory_path_to_url(absolute_path.to_path())
   232	                    .map_err(|_err| ParseChannelError::InvalidPath(channel.to_owned()))?;
   233	                Self {
   234	                    platforms,
   235	                    base_url: url.into(),
   236	                    name: Some(channel.to_owned()),
   237	                }
   238	            }
   239	        } else {
   240	            // Validate that the channel is a valid name
   241	            if channel.contains([':', '\\']) {
   242	                return Err(ParseChannelError::InvalidName(channel.to_owned()));
   243	            }
   244	            Channel {
   245	                platforms,
   246	                ..Channel::from_name(channel, config)
   247	            }
   248	        };
   249	
   250	        Ok(channel)
   251	    }
   252	
   253	    /// Set the explicit platforms of the channel.
   254	    pub fn with_explicit_platforms(self, platforms: impl IntoIterator<Item = Platform>) -> Self {
   255	        Self {
   256	            platforms: Some(platforms.into_iter().collect()),
   257	            ..self
   258	        }
   259	    }
   260	
   261	    /// Constructs a new [`Channel`] from a `Url` and associated platforms.
   262	    pub fn from_url(url: impl Into<ChannelUrl>) -> Self {
   263	        // Get the path part of the URL but trim the directory suffix
   264	        let url: ChannelUrl = url.into();
   265	
   266	        let path = url.url().path().trim_end_matches('/');
   267	
   268	        // Case 1: No path give, channel name is ""
   269	
   270	        // Case 2: migrated_custom_channels

1. In `features_ext.rs` → `exclude_newer_config_resolved()` → creates `ResolvedExcludeNewer` with channel cutoffs keyed by `channel.channel.to_string()`
2. This is converted to `rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer` via `From<ResolvedExcludeNewer>`
3. The solver uses `record.channel` (which is the base URL) to look up channel cutoffs

```rust
fn prioritized_channels(
    &self,
) -> IndexMap<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl, &'source PrioritizedChannel> {
```

The key is `&'source NamedChannelOrUrl`, and the value is `&'source PrioritizedChannel`. Then in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`:

```rust
for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
    let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
        continue;
    };
    ...
    *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
        ...
        .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
    };
}
```

So `channel` here is a `&PrioritizedChannel`, and `channel.channel` is a `NamedChannelOrUrl`. `channel.channel.to_string()` uses the `Display` impl which calls `as_str()`.

For URL channels: `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`


It's an exact match. So named channels would NOT match their resolved URLs.

But maybe there's a different path for the PR issue. Let me think about this differently.

The PR says:
- workspace `exclude-newer = "1y"` 
- channel `{ channel = "https://prefix.dev/github-releases", exclude-newer = "2d" }`
- The channel-level `2d` doesn't work because workspace-level `1y` takes precedence

In this case, both the channel name and the channel key are URLs. `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` (from `NamedChannelOrUrl::to_string()`) vs `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` (from `channel.base_url.url().redact().to_string()`). The difference might be trailing slash.

But actually, the PR description says the error shows a cutoff of `2025-04-08`, which is about 1 year before now (March 2026). The workspace-level is `1y` and the channel-level is `2d`. If the channel-level were working, the cutoff for `github-releases` would be 2 days ago, not 1 year ago.

So the channel-level override is NOT being applied at all. This means the channel key matching fails.

Let me look more carefully at the `exclude_newer_config_resolved` method. Maybe the issue is simpler: the workspace-level `exclude_newer` is being set AFTER the channel-level overrides are applied?

Look at the code again:

```rust
let mut exclude_newer = self
    .exclude_newer_raw()
    .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));

for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
    ...
    let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
        ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
    });
    *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
        ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(...),
        ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(...),
    };
}
```

Line 1-3: If workspace-level `exclude_newer` exists, set `exclude_newer` to a `ResolvedExcludeNewer` with the workspace cutoff as default.

Then for each channel with channel-level `exclude_newer`, it adds a channel-specific cutoff.

The issue might be that `with_channel_cutoff` creates a NEW `ResolvedExcludeNewer` with the channel cutoff added, but the default cutoff is preserved from the original. So:

1. Start with `ResolvedExcludeNewer { cutoff: 2025-04-08, channel_cutoffs: {}, package_cutoffs: {} }` (workspace `1y`)
2. For `github-releases` channel: `ResolvedExcludeNewer { cutoff: 2025-04-08, channel_cutoffs: {"https://prefix.dev/github-releases": 2d_ago}, ... }`

This should work: the channel cutoff for `github-releases` is `2d_ago`, which is more permissive than the default `1y_ago`.

But the error shows that the cutoff is `2025-04-08` (1 year ago), not 2 days ago. So the channel cutoff is either not being applied or the key doesn't match.

Let me check if there's an issue with how `channel.channel.to_string()` works for a URL. For `NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url)`, `as_str()` returns `url.as_str().trim_end_matches('/')`. So `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` (without trailing slash). But the record's channel might be `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` (with trailing slash). That would cause a mismatch.

But wait, let me look at the redact function and how the channel name is set in `parse_records_raw`:

```rust
let channel_name = channel.base_url.url().clone().redact().to_string();
```

`redact()` likely removes sensitive info like passwords. For most URLs, it would return the same URL. And the URL from `channel.base_url` would be the resolved base URL, which for `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` would be `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` (with trailing slash).

So the keys don't match: `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` vs `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"`.

But wait, I should also check for named channels. For `conda-forge`, the channel key would be `"conda-forge"` (from `NamedChannelOrUrl::Name("conda-forge").to_string()`), but the record's channel would be `"https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/"` (the resolved URL). These DEFINITELY don't match.

Hmm, but the tests pass. Let me look at the test more carefully:

In `test_channel_specific_exclude_newer_across_multiple_features`, the channels are:
- `[project] channels = ["conda-forge"]` — workspace channels
- `[feature.foo] channels = [{ channel = "bioconda", exclude-newer = ... }, ...]` — feature channels

The test directly calls `config.cutoff_for_package(&package, Some("bioconda"))`. This passes `"bioconda"` as the channel string, which matches the key stored in channel_cutoffs (`"bioconda"`).

So the test works because it manually passes the channel name. But in the real solver path, the channel passed is the base URL, not the name. This means the channel cutoff feature might be BROKEN for all named channels, and only works for URL channels where the URL happens to match.

Wait, let me check the test `test_exclude_newer_package_channel_workspace_precedence` again. It uses:
```
channels = [{ channel = "my-private-forge", exclude-newer = "2016-12-02T02:07:43Z" }, "conda-forge"]
```

And checks:
```rust

assert_eq!(private_numpy_cutoff, channel_cutoff);
```

Again, it passes `"my-private-forge"` directly. So the tests bypass the real channel resolution.

So the bug is that the channel keys stored in `channel_cutoffs` are the "raw" channel names/URLs from the manifest, but the channel strings that come from repodata records are the resolved base URLs. They don't match for named channels.

But hold on, maybe for URL channels (like `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`), the resolved URL is the same? Let me check how a URL channel is resolved.

For `NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url)` where `url = "https://prefix.dev/github-releases"`:
- `into_base_url` would return `ChannelUrl::from(url)` (with trailing slash added)
- But the key used in channel_cutoffs is `channel.channel.to_string()` which returns `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` (no trailing slash)
- The record's channel would be `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` (with trailing slash)

So even for URL channels, the keys might not match due to trailing slash differences.

Now, let me think about the fix. The channel key used for matching should be the same as what the solver sees. Looking at `parse_records_raw`:

```rust
let channel_name = channel.base_url.url().clone().redact().to_string();
```

This is the base URL string. So we should use the resolved base URL as the key in `channel_cutoffs`, not the raw `NamedChannelOrUrl::to_string()`.

But wait, in `features_ext.rs`, we're in the manifest layer, and we might not have access to the `ChannelConfig` to resolve the URLs. However, the `FeaturesExt` trait has `channel_urls` method that resolves URLs. But we need the string representation of the channel as it would appear in `record.channel`.

Actually, looking at `exclude_newer_config_resolved`, it takes `&self` and returns `Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError>`. The `ParseChannelError` suggests it might need channel config. But currently it doesn't use it.

Let me look at how channels are resolved elsewhere. In `channel_urls`:

```rust
fn channel_urls(
    &self,
    channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
) -> Result<Vec<ChannelUrl>, ParseChannelError> {
    self.channels()
        .into_iter()
        .cloned()
        .map(|channel| channel.into_base_url(channel_config))
        .collect()
}
```

So to get the base URL for a channel, we need `ChannelConfig`. But `exclude_newer_config_resolved` doesn't take a `ChannelConfig`.

Maybe the fix should be: use the `NamedChannelOrUrl::into_base_url` to get the proper URL string and use that as the key. But we'd need `ChannelConfig` for that.

Alternatively, maybe the fix is simpler: the issue is that the workspace-level `exclude-newer` should NOT set a global default when channel-level settings are present. Instead, when channel-level settings exist, they should provide the default for those channels, and the workspace-level should only apply to channels WITHOUT a channel-level setting.

But looking at the PR description:
> It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` IMO.

This means: workspace-level should override channel-level, channel-level should override package-level. Wait, that's the opposite. Let me re-read:

"`workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]`"

The `>` here might mean "takes precedence over". So:
- `workspace.exclude-newer` (highest priority, overrides everything)
- `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` (next priority)
- `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` (lowest priority, overridden by above)

But the PR says:
> The `workspace.exclude-newer` overrides the channel version, which I believe to be wrong.

So the user believes workspace-level should NOT override channel-level. They want channel-level to take precedence over workspace-level.

Actually, wait. Let me re-read the expected behavior:
> It should be `workspace.exclude-newer` > `workspace.channels.exclude-newer` > `[(pypi-)exclude-newer]` IMO.

The `>` might mean "greater than" in terms of scope (broader scope), not precedence. Or it could mean the order in which they are applied.

Given the PR title: "[exclude-newer]: Workspace level takes precedence over channel"

So the issue is that workspace-level takes precedence over channel-level, but the user expects channel-level to take precedence over workspace-level.

Looking at the current code:
1. Workspace-level sets the default `cutoff`
2. Channel-level adds `channel_cutoffs` which override the default for specific channels

So currently: if workspace `exclude-newer = "1y"` and channel `exclude-newer = "2d"`, the default cutoff is `1y` and the channel override is `2d`. The channel override takes precedence for that specific channel. So channel-level DOES take precedence over workspace-level for that channel.

But the PR says it doesn't work. So either:
1. The channel keys don't match (as I suspected above), or
2. There's a different code path issue

     7	
     8	/// Represents a channel base url. This is a wrapper around an url that is
     9	/// normalized:
    10	///
    11	/// * The URL always contains a trailing `/`.
    12	///
    13	/// This is useful to be able to compare different channels.
    14	#[derive(Clone, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Deserialize)]
    15	#[serde(transparent)]
    16	pub struct ChannelUrl(UrlWithTrailingSlash);
    17	
    18	impl ChannelUrl {
    19	    /// Returns the base Url of the channel.
    20	    pub fn url(&self) -> &UrlWithTrailingSlash {
    21	        &self.0
    22	    }
    23	
    24	    /// Returns the string representation of the url.
    25	    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
    26	        self.0.as_str()
    27	    }
    28	
    29	    /// Append the platform to the base url.
    30	    pub fn platform_url(&self, platform: Platform) -> Url {
    31	        self.0
    32	            .join(&format!("{}/", platform.as_str())) // trailing slash is important here as this signifies a directory
    33	            .expect("platform is a valid url fragment")
    34	    }
    35	}
    36	
    37	// Override the behavior of the `Serialize` trait to remove the trailing slash.
    38	// In code, we want to ensure that a trailing slash is always present but when
    39	// we serialize the type it can be removed to safe space and make it look better
    40	// for humans.
    41	impl Serialize for ChannelUrl {
    42	    fn serialize<S>(&self, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error>
    43	    where
    44	        S: serde::Serializer,
    45	    {
    46	        self.as_str().trim_end_matches('/').serialize(serializer)
    47	    }
    48	}
    49	
    50	impl Debug for ChannelUrl {
    51	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
    52	        write!(f, "{}", self.0.as_str())
    53	    }
    54	}
    55	
    56	impl From<Url> for ChannelUrl {
    57	    fn from(url: Url) -> Self {
    58	        Self(UrlWithTrailingSlash::from(url))
    59	    }
    60	}
    61	
    62	impl From<ChannelUrl> for Url {
    63	    fn from(value: ChannelUrl) -> Self {
    64	        value.0.into()
    65	    }
    66	}
    67	
    68	impl AsRef<Url> for ChannelUrl {
    69	    fn as_ref(&self) -> &Url {
    70	        &self.0
    71	    }
    72	}
    73

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /usr/local/cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/rattler_conda_types-0.44.4/src/channel/mod.rs:
    80	    }

    82	
    83	/// Represents a channel description as either a name (e.g. `conda-forge`) or a
    84	/// base url.
    85	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]
    86	pub enum NamedChannelOrUrl {
    87	    /// A named channel
    88	    Name(String),
    89	
    90	    /// A url
    91	    Url(Url),
    92	
    93	    /// A path. Can either be absolute or relative.
    94	    Path(Utf8TypedPathBuf),
    95	}
    96	
    97	impl NamedChannelOrUrl {
    98	    /// Returns the string representation of the channel.
    99	    ///
   100	    /// This method ensures that if the channel is a url, it does not end with a
   101	    /// `/`.
   102	    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
   103	        match self {
   104	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name) => name,
   105	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url) => url.as_str().trim_end_matches('/'),
   106	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Path(path) => path.as_str(),
   107	        }
   108	    }
   109	
   110	    /// Converts the channel to a base url using the given configuration.
   111	    /// This method ensures that the base url always ends with a `/`.
   112	    pub fn into_base_url(self, config: &ChannelConfig) -> Result<ChannelUrl, ParseChannelError> {
   113	        let url = match self {
   114	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name) => {
   115	                let mut base_url = config.channel_alias.clone();
   116	                if let Ok(mut segments) = base_url.path_segments_mut() {
   117	                    for segment in name.split(&['/', '\\']) {
   118	                        segments.push(segment);
   119	                    }
   120	                }
   121	                base_url.into()
   122	            }
   123	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url) => url.into(),
   124	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Path(path) => {
   125	                let absolute_path = absolute_path(path.as_str(), &config.root_dir)?;
   126	                directory_path_to_url(absolute_path.to_path())
   127	                    .map_err(|_err| ParseChannelError::InvalidPath(path.to_string()))?
   128	                    .into()
   129	            }
   130	        };
   131	        Ok(url)
   132	    }
   133	
   134	    /// Converts this instance into a channel.
   135	    pub fn into_channel(self, config: &ChannelConfig) -> Result<Channel, ParseChannelError> {
   136	        let name = match &self {
   137	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name) => Some(name.clone()),
   138	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(base_url) => config.strip_channel_alias(base_url),
   139	            NamedChannelOrUrl::Path(_) => None,
   140	        };

The `into_base_url` method resolves a named channel to its full URL (e.g., `"conda-forge"` → `"https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/"`), and a URL channel to itself with trailing slash (e.g., `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` → `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"`).

The `channel_name` in the record (from `parse_records_raw`) is:
```rust
let channel_name = channel.base_url.url().clone().redact().to_string();
```

Which is the full URL with trailing slash.

So the fix should be: in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`, instead of using `channel.channel.to_string()` as the key, we should use the resolved base URL (via `into_base_url`). But we need `ChannelConfig` for that, and the method doesn't have it.

But wait, there's another way to look at this. Let me check the `HasWorkspaceManifest` trait to see if we can get `ChannelConfig`:

The tests all use named channels (like `"bioconda"`, `"conda-forge"`, `"my-private-forge"`) and pass the same names to `cutoff_for_package`. No tests use URL-based channels.

Now I'm fairly confident the bug is about channel key mismatch. But let me also check if there's perhaps a different issue: maybe the `exclude_newer_config_resolved` method doesn't properly handle the case where both workspace-level and channel-level exclude-newer are set.

Let me trace through the code again with the PR example:

```toml
[workspace]
channels = [
    { channel = "conda-forge", exclude-newer = "3y"},
    { channel = "bioconda", exclude-newer = "1y"},
    { channel = "https://prefix.dev/github-releases", exclude-newer = "2d"}
]
platforms = ["osx-arm64", ...]
exclude-newer = "1y"
```

1. `exclude_newer_raw()` returns `Some(ExcludeNewer::Duration(1 year))` → cutoff = `now - 1 year` ≈ `2025-04-08`
2. `exclude_newer` starts as `Some(ResolvedExcludeNewer { cutoff: 2025-04-08, channel_cutoffs: {}, package_cutoffs: {} })`
3. For channel `conda-forge` with `exclude-newer = "3y"`: adds channel cutoff `"conda-forge"` → `now - 3y` ≈ `2023-04-08`
4. For channel `bioconda` with `exclude-newer = "1y"`: adds channel cutoff `"bioconda"` → `now - 1y` ≈ `2025-04-08`
5. For channel `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` with `exclude-newer = "2d"`: adds channel cutoff `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` → `now - 2d` ≈ `2026-04-06` (assuming now is 2026-04-08)

Now in the solver, when checking a package from `github-releases`:
- `record.channel` is something like `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases/"` (with trailing slash)
- The lookup in `channel_cutoffs` is for `"https://prefix.dev/github-releases"` (without trailing slash)
- They don't match! So the default cutoff (`2025-04-08`) is used.

That's the bug! The channel key mismatch causes the channel-level override to be silently ignored.

Now, the fix should be in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`. Instead of using `channel.channel.to_string()` (which returns the raw name/URL from the manifest), we should use the resolved base URL (with trailing slash) that matches what the solver sees.

But `exclude_newer_config_resolved` doesn't have access to `ChannelConfig`. However, looking at the callers:

1. Add `ChannelConfig` parameter to `exclude_newer_config_resolved`
2. Or find another way to get the resolved URL

```rust
fn channel_urls(
    &self,
    channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
) -> Result<Vec<ChannelUrl>, ParseChannelError> {
    self.channels()
        .into_iter()
        .cloned()
        .map(|channel| channel.into_base_url(channel_config))
        .collect()
}
```

It resolves channels using `into_base_url`. So we could modify `exclude_newer_config_resolved` to also accept a `ChannelConfig` and resolve the channel URLs.

Yes, `channel_config` is available at this call site. But the current `exclude_newer_config_resolved` doesn't take it.

Now, let me think about the proper fix. The issue is that `channel.channel.to_string()` returns the raw channel name/URL as written in the manifest, but the solver uses the resolved base URL (with trailing slash). We need to resolve the channel URL before using it as a key.

But actually, looking at this more carefully, I think the issue might be more fundamental. Let me look at how `prioritized_channels` works again:

```rust
fn prioritized_channels(
    &self,
) -> IndexMap<&'source NamedChannelOrUrl, &'source PrioritizedChannel> {
    let channels = self.features().flat_map(|feature| match &feature.channels {
        Some(channels) => channels,
        None => &self.workspace_manifest().workspace.channels,
    });

    let mut prioritized = IndexMap::new();
    for channel in channels.into_iter().sorted_by(|a, b| {
        let a = a.priority.unwrap_or(0);
        let b = b.priority.unwrap_or(0);
        b.cmp(&a)
    }) {
        prioritized.entry(&channel.channel).or_insert(channel);
    }

    prioritized
}
```

The key is `&NamedChannelOrUrl` and the value is `&PrioritizedChannel`. The `NamedChannelOrUrl` is the raw channel as written in the manifest.

Then in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`:
```rust
for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
    ...
    .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
}
```

So it uses `channel.channel.to_string()` as the key. This is the raw channel name/URL.

But the solver's `record.channel` is the resolved base URL. So they don't match.

Now, what's the proper fix? We need to ensure the channel keys match. Options:

1. Change `exclude_newer_config_resolved` to accept a `ChannelConfig` and resolve the channel URLs before using them as keys.

2. Change the solver-side code to use the raw channel name instead of the resolved base URL.

3. Change both sides to use a canonical form.

Option 1 seems most appropriate since the method already returns `Result<..., ParseChannelError>`, suggesting it might need to do channel resolution.

Let me look at the trait definition and all callers to understand the impact of changing the signature:

Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the code in `features_ext.rs`. The method `exclude_newer_config_resolved` is on `FeaturesExt` which requires `HasWorkspaceManifest`. But `HasWorkspaceManifest` only provides `workspace_manifest()`. It doesn't provide `channel_config()`.

However, looking at the `Workspace` struct (in `pixi_manifest`), it doesn't have a `channel_config` method. The `ChannelConfig` is on the higher-level `Workspace` in `pixi_core`.

So we can't easily add `ChannelConfig` to `exclude_newer_config_resolved` without changing the trait bounds.

But wait, maybe there's a simpler fix. Let me look at how `channel_urls` is implemented:

```rust
fn channel_urls(
    &self,
    channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
) -> Result<Vec<ChannelUrl>, ParseChannelError> {
    self.channels()
        .into_iter()
        .cloned()
        .map(|channel| channel.into_base_url(channel_config))
        .collect()
}
```

It takes a `ChannelConfig`. But `exclude_newer_config_resolved` doesn't.

Maybe the fix should be: instead of using `channel.channel.to_string()` as the key, we should use something that matches what the solver sees. But what does the solver see?

In `parse_records_raw`:
```rust
let channel_name = channel.base_url.url().clone().redact().to_string();
```

`channel.base_url.url()` returns `&UrlWithTrailingSlash`. `.clone().redact().to_string()` produces the URL string with trailing slash.

And in `into_base_url` for a URL channel:
```rust
NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url) => url.into(),
```

Where `url.into()` creates a `ChannelUrl` which wraps `UrlWithTrailingSlash`. So the URL is stored with a trailing slash.

For a named channel:
```rust
NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name) => {
    let mut base_url = config.channel_alias.clone();
    if let Ok(mut segments) = base_url.path_segments_mut() {
        for segment in name.split(&['/', '\\']) {
            segments.push(segment);
        }
    }
    base_url.into()
}
```

This creates a URL like `"https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/"` (with trailing slash).

So the solver sees URLs with trailing slashes. But `channel.channel.to_string()` for URL channels returns the URL WITHOUT trailing slash (because `as_str()` trims it). For named channels, it returns just the name.

So the fix should make the keys match the resolved base URLs. But since we don't have `ChannelConfig` in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`, maybe we need to add it.

Alternatively, maybe we should change the approach: instead of resolving channels in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`, we could change the solver side to normalize channel names before lookup. But that's in the rattler library which we may not want to modify.

```rust
pub fn cutoff_for_package(
    &self,
    package: &PackageName,
    channel: Option<&str>,
) -> DateTime<Utc> {
    self.package_cutoffs
        .get(package)
        .copied()
        .or_else(|| channel.and_then(|channel| self.channel_cutoffs.get(channel).copied()))
        .unwrap_or(self.cutoff)
}
```

So it does an exact match on the channel string. If the keys don't match, the channel override is silently ignored.

```rust
pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
    match self {
        NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name) => name,
        NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url) => url.as_str().trim_end_matches('/'),
        NamedChannelOrUrl::Path(path) => path.as_str(),
    }
}
```

For URLs, it trims trailing slashes. But the solver uses trailing slashes.

What if we change the key to include a trailing slash for URL channels? That would fix the URL case. For named channels, we'd still have a mismatch, but maybe we can fix that by also changing how the solver stores the channel for named channels.

The PR title says: "[exclude-newer]: Workspace level takes precedence over channel"

The user expects: channel-level `exclude-newer` should override workspace-level `exclude-newer`. Currently, workspace-level takes precedence.

But looking at the code, channel-level SHOULD override workspace-level (because channel cutoffs override the default cutoff). The issue is that the channel keys don't match, so the channel override is silently ignored and the workspace default is used.

So the fix should ensure channel keys match. Let me now implement the fix.

First, let me check if there's a way to get the "matching" channel string from a `NamedChannelOrUrl` without `ChannelConfig`. For URL channels, we can just ensure trailing slash. For named channels... we might need to accept that they won't match and require `ChannelConfig`.

But wait, maybe the fix should be at a different level. Let me look at how the solver task is built:

In `solve_conda/mod.rs`:
```rust
let exclude_newer = self.exclude_newer.clone().map(Into::into);
```

And `self.exclude_newer` is `Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>`. This is converted to `rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer` via `From`. 

In `features_ext.rs`:
```rust
impl From<ResolvedExcludeNewer> for rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer {
    fn from(value: ResolvedExcludeNewer) -> Self {
        let mut config = rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer::from_datetime(value.cutoff)
            .with_include_unknown_timestamp(value.include_unknown_timestamp);

        for (channel, cutoff) in value.channel_cutoffs {
            config = config.with_channel_cutoff(channel, cutoff);
        }

        for (package, cutoff) in value.package_cutoffs {
            config = config.with_package_cutoff(package, cutoff);
        }

        config
    }
}
```

So the channel keys are passed through as-is. The solver then does an exact match against `record.channel`.

Now, let me think about what the correct key should be. The solver uses `record.channel` which is the base URL with trailing slash. So the key should be the base URL with trailing slash.

For `NamedChannelOrUrl::Url(url)`, the base URL is `url` with trailing slash. We can compute this without `ChannelConfig`.

For `NamedChannelOrUrl::Name(name)`, the base URL depends on `ChannelConfig` (the channel alias). So we need `ChannelConfig`.

For `NamedChannelOrUrl::Path(path)`, similar issue.

So, one approach: change `exclude_newer_config_resolved` to accept a `&ChannelConfig` and resolve all channels to their base URLs. This is the most correct fix.

Let me see how many places call `exclude_newer_config_resolved` and whether they have access to `ChannelConfig`:

1. `crates/pixi_core/src/lock_file/update.rs:2267` - has `channel_config`

3. `crates/pixi_core/src/lock_file/satisfiability/mod.rs:162` - uses `exclude_newer_config()` which wraps `exclude_newer_config_resolved()`. The `Environment` has `channel_config()` method.
4. Tests - can use a default `ChannelConfig`

```rust
fn pypi_exclude_newer_config_resolved(&self) -> ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer {
    let mut exclude_newer = self
        .exclude_newer_raw()
        .map(|config| ResolvedPypiExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()))
        .unwrap_or_default();

    for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
        .workspace_manifest()
        .workspace
        .pypi_exclude_newer_package_overrides
    {
        // ...
    }

    exclude_newer
}
```

The pypi version doesn't iterate over channels, only package overrides. So it's not affected.

There's only one blanket implementation. So any type that implements both `HasWorkspaceManifest` and `HasFeaturesIter` gets `FeaturesExt`.

Now, let me implement the fix. The key change is in `exclude_newer_config_resolved`:

1. Add a `channel_config: &ChannelConfig` parameter
2. For each channel with `exclude_newer`, resolve the channel to its base URL and use that as the key

Let me also check if there's a method to get the string representation of a `ChannelUrl` that matches what the solver uses:

In `parse_records_raw`:
```rust
let channel_name = channel.base_url.url().clone().redact().to_string();
```

`channel.base_url` is `ChannelUrl`. `channel.base_url.url()` returns `&UrlWithTrailingSlash`. `.clone().redact().to_string()` returns the URL string (with trailing slash, since `UrlWithTrailingSlash` implements `Display` which includes the trailing slash).

So we need to use `ChannelUrl`'s display string (which has trailing slash) as the key.

Now let me also check: for `NamedChannelOrUrl::into_base_url`, the result is a `ChannelUrl`. The `Display` of `ChannelUrl` includes the trailing slash. So if we call `into_base_url` and then convert to string, we get the URL with trailing slash.

   123	    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
   124	    fn exclude_newer_config_resolved(
   125	        &self,
   126	    ) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
   127	        let mut exclude_newer = self
   128	            .exclude_newer_raw()
   129	            .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));
   130	
   131	        for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
   132	            let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
   133	                continue;
   134	            };
   135	
   136	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
   137	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
   138	            });
   139	
   140	            *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
   141	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
   142	                    .clone()
   143	                    .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
   144	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
   145	                    channel.channel.to_string(),
   146	                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
   147	                ),
   148	            };
   149	        }
   150	
   151	        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
   152	            .workspace_manifest()
   153	            .workspace
   154	            .exclude_newer_package_overrides
   155	        {
   156	            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
   157	                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
   158	            });
   159	
   160	            *config = match package_exclude_newer {
   161	                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
   162	                    config.clone().with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), *dt)
   163	                }
   164	                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config
   165	                    .clone()
   166	                    .with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff()),
   167	            };
   168	        }
   169	
   170	        Ok(exclude_newer)
   171	    }

Now, I think the cleanest fix is to change `exclude_newer_config_resolved` to take a `&ChannelConfig`. Let me trace the impact:

1. The trait method signature changes
2. The blanket impl changes
3. All callers need to pass `ChannelConfig`

But some callers might not have easy access to `ChannelConfig`. Let me check all callers:

- `crates/pixi_core/src/lock_file/update.rs:2267`: `group.exclude_newer_config_resolved()` → `group` is a `GroupedEnvironment`, which has `channel_config()`
- `crates/pixi_core/src/environment/conda_prefix.rs:76`: `self.group.exclude_newer_config_resolved()` → `self.group` is a `GroupedEnvironment`, which has `channel_config()`
- `crates/pixi_core/src/lock_file/satisfiability/mod.rs:162`: `environment.exclude_newer_config()` → `environment` is an `Environment`, which has `channel_config()`
- `crates/pixi_core/src/workspace/environment.rs:836, 901, 954`: tests
- `crates/pixi_manifest/src/manifests/workspace.rs:3553, 3596`: tests

For the tests, we can use a default `ChannelConfig`.

But wait, there's also `exclude_newer_config` which wraps `exclude_newer_config_resolved`. Both are trait methods. Changing `exclude_newer_config_resolved` to take `ChannelConfig` means `exclude_newer_config` also needs it (or we change its implementation to not use `exclude_newer_config_resolved`).

Let me look at `exclude_newer_config`:

```rust
fn exclude_newer_config(
    &self,
) -> Result<Option<rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
    self.exclude_newer_config_resolved()
        .map(|exclude_newer| exclude_newer.map(Into::into))
}
```

It just calls `exclude_newer_config_resolved`. If we change the signature of `exclude_newer_config_resolved`, we need to change `exclude_newer_config` too.

OK, let me just look at how many callers of `exclude_newer_config` there are:

From the grep above, `exclude_newer_config` is called at:
- `crates/pixi_core/src/lock_file/satisfiability/mod.rs:162`
- `crates/pixi_manifest/src/manifests/workspace.rs:3553, 3596` (tests)

So there are fewer callers of `exclude_newer_config` than `exclude_newer_config_resolved`.

Let me now implement the changes. I'll:

1. Modify `exclude_newer_config_resolved` to take `channel_config: &ChannelConfig`
2. Modify `exclude_newer_config` to take `channel_config: &ChannelConfig`

    /// package-specific overrides are applied.
    fn exclude_newer_raw(&self) -> Option<ExcludeNewer> {
        self.workspace_manifest().workspace.exclude_newer
    }

    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration.
    fn exclude_newer_config(
        &self,
    ) -> Result<Option<rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
        self.exclude_newer_config_resolved()
            .map(|exclude_newer| exclude_newer.map(Into::into))
    }

    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
    fn exclude_newer_config_resolved(
        &self,
    ) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
        let mut exclude_newer = self
            .exclude_newer_raw()
            .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));

        for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
            let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
                continue;
            };

            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
            });

            *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
                    .clone()
                    .with_channel_cutoff(channel.channel.to_string(), dt),
                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
                    channel.channel.to_string(),
                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
                ),
            };
        }

        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
            .workspace_manifest()
            .workspace
            .exclude_newer_package_overrides
        {
            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
            });

            *config = match package_exclude_newer {
                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
                    config.clone().with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), *dt)
                }
                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config
                    .clone()
                    .with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff()),
            };
        }

        Ok(exclude_newer)
    }' --new_str '/// Returns the raw workspace exclude-newer configuration before channel and
    /// package-specific overrides are applied.
    fn exclude_newer_raw(&self) -> Option<ExcludeNewer> {
        self.workspace_manifest().workspace.exclude_newer
    }

    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration.
    fn exclude_newer_config(
        &self,
        channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
    ) -> Result<Option<rattler_solve::ExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
        self.exclude_newer_config_resolved(channel_config)
            .map(|exclude_newer| exclude_newer.map(Into::into))
    }

    /// Returns the effective exclude-newer solver configuration with absolute cutoffs.
    fn exclude_newer_config_resolved(
        &self,
        channel_config: &ChannelConfig,
    ) -> Result<Option<ResolvedExcludeNewer>, ParseChannelError> {
        let mut exclude_newer = self
            .exclude_newer_raw()
            .map(|config| ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(config.cutoff()));

        for channel in self.prioritized_channels().into_values() {
            let Some(channel_exclude_newer) = channel.exclude_newer else {
                continue;
            };

            // Resolve the channel to its base URL so that the key matches what the
            // solver sees in `record.channel`.
            let channel_key = channel
                .channel
                .clone()
                .into_base_url(channel_config)?
                .as_str()
                .to_owned();

            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
            });

            *config = match channel_exclude_newer {
                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => config
                    .clone()
                    .with_channel_cutoff(channel_key.clone(), dt),
                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config.clone().with_channel_cutoff(
                    channel_key.clone(),
                    ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration).cutoff(),
                ),
            };
        }

        for (name, package_exclude_newer) in &self
            .workspace_manifest()
            .workspace
            .exclude_newer_package_overrides
        {
            let config = exclude_newer.get_or_insert_with(|| {
                ResolvedExcludeNewer::from_datetime(DateTime::<Utc>::MAX_UTC)
            });

            *config = match package_exclude_newer {
                ExcludeNewer::Timestamp(dt) => {
                    config.clone().with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), *dt)
                }
                ExcludeNewer::Duration(duration) => config
                    .clone()
                    .with_package_cutoff(name.clone(), ExcludeNewer::Duration(*duration).cutoff()),
            };
        }

        Ok(exclude_newer)
    }'

   267	    pub fn from_str(manifest_path: &Path, content: &str) -> Result<Self, LoadManifestsError> {
   268	        let WithWarnings {
   269	            value: manifests, ..
   270	        } = Manifests::from_workspace_source(
   271	            content.with_provenance(ManifestProvenance::from_path(manifest_path.to_path_buf())?),
   272	        )?;
   273	        Ok(Self::from_manifests(manifests))
   274	    }

   313	    /// If the name of the root directory could not be determined, "workspace"
